reviews.text: Fantastic firm! I was kept informed and supported throughout the entire process. I was also updated as to my responsibilities as well as everyone else's at all times. I hope I don't need a lawyer any time soon, but if I do I will be coming right back to this firm.

After a year of battling with DA's office and finally going to trial I was found NOT GUILTY. Very satisfied with there service. I'm a free woman and now I can move forward with my life. As a fellow plaintiff’s attorney, I’ve had the privilege of going to trial alongside Sanga Turnbull, and I can confidently say he is one of the most skilled and passionate advocates for justice I’ve encountered. His dedication to representing those who have been harmed, particularly in cases involving nursing home negligence and abuse, is truly inspiring.

Sanga’s trial skills are exceptional—his ability to present a compelling case, connect with juries, and fight tirelessly for his clients’ rights sets him apart. He has an unparalleled work ethic and a deep understanding of personal injury law in both Florida and Georgia, which enables him to navigate even the most complex cases with precision and confidence.

Beyond his legal expertise, Sanga is someone of tremendous personal character. He is empathetic, approachable, and genuinely cares about his clients and their families. It’s clear that his motivation comes from a true desire to make a difference in their lives.

If you’re seeking an attorney who will not only deliver outstanding results but also stand by you with integrity and compassion, Sanga Turnbull is the one to call. I recommend him without hesitation and with the utmost respect
reviews.ownerResponse: Appreciated.   Couldn’t ask for a better person at trial

reviews.text:
I cannot thank Attorney Turnbull enough for his incredible support and expertise after I was involved in a truck accident that left me with a serious neck injury. The physical pain was only compounded by the overwhelming stress of mounting medical expenses and the inability to work. I was unsure how I would manage everything, but Attorney Turnbull made the entire process not only manageable but survivable.

From the beginning, he listened carefully to my concerns and took the time to explain each step of the legal process, relieving much of my stress. His aggressive approach to negotiating with the insurance companies helped ensure that I received the compensation I deserved for my medical expenses and lost wages. Attorney Turnbull was a true advocate for me every step of the way, making sure I had the resources to heal and move forward. If you’re dealing with a truck accident and its aftermath, I highly recommend Attorney Turnbull—he’s a game changer!
Without a doubt, one of the best law firms in Florida and Georgia! The staff and the attorney show great expertise, professionalism, and dedication, making them stand out from the rest.

He handled everything with precision and dedication, ensuring that every aspect of my case was thoroughly investigated and that I got the compensation I deserved. In June 2023, my college friend and I were involved in an ugly accident with an 18 wheeler truck carrying a military tank. My Toyota corrola was no match. The 18 W truck, clipped my driver side back wheel and spun us onto the truck's front engine, we then spun off to the left and eventually slammed into the median wall trunk side first. Two separate women stopped to assist us on the fast lane side (amazing). It was a very tramatic experience. Praise God, we were both eventually able to leave the site , thinking without serious injury. We did our best to document everything and fully cooperate with the police officers on the scene.  There is so much to the entire situation that I could expound on, but what I  to highlight us that my shoulder surgery went well, the treatment I received for my back and knee were excellent and my attorney saw to that from day 1.  There was a lot of pain suffered and many frustrated nights having to use a walker to use the bathroom.  My faith and patience were tested on a daily basis. Tyler was superb and also patient with answering every call, every question, not only for myself, but for the passenger in the car, my good friend from California.  So eventually, he pulled double duty.  Tyler walked us both through every aspect of the case.  He was extremely knowledgeable, dependable, funny, courteous....I could keep going.  He excelled at his job and his communication was that he was there to see that we were compensated for our what we lost over the last year.  I can't thank him and his team enough. I highly recommend The Schermerhorn Firm and my guy Tyler is an excellent attorney and is a position to get the job done.  Don't believe me, hire him and then call me.  Many Blessings and Happy Holidays Tyler. Again  Thank you from me and my family.

reviews.text: Was extremely knowledgeable and helpful.

Ms. Sullivan was absolutely amazing to work with. From the start, she was thorough in her guidance and communication — she took the time to explain all of my options clearly, and together we made a solid plan that truly worked. She’s not only knowledgeable and professional but also genuinely committed to her clients. She took on the insurance company with confidence and skill, and thanks to her hard work, I received a great settlement. I can’t say enough about how good she is at what she does. Highly recommend!
She’s amazing !!!!! If you need help with workers comp she’s the best lawyer to consult very genuine nice and she knows what she’s doing !!!
Elizabeth is an excellent attorney and I would happily trust her to handle any workers' comp case.

Elizabeth Sullivan isn’t just a great lawyer, she’s a great human - which is not something I think most people think of when they think of a lawyer.

This was my first time getting a lawyer on my own, and I had met with several before I got to Elizabeth. It’s really stressful when you’re going through such a difficult time to have to pick someone to be beside you and trust to help you off of only a few conversations and their general vibe.

I ultimately picked Elizabeth because she made me feel cared for as a human, not just a client. She really listened to me, made me feel understood, and empathized with my situation. She didn’t talk to me like I should already know something or like I didn’t know anything, and she wasn’t pushy, as so many lawyers have the reputation of being.

It was the best choice I could have made because she was really beside me every step of my case, and saw me through to the end despite the endless twists and turns we encountered. She wasn’t there for me just as a client, she was there for me as a human being and it was something that I wasn’t expecting but what I really needed during such a difficult time.

I feel really lucky to have found Elizabeth and am so grateful she took my case and helped me. I genuinely feel like given my unique circumstances she helped me get the best possible outcome for myself, and I was able to come out of this rough patch better on the other side.

If you are looking for a lawyer, I would highly recommend Elizabeth Sullivan. I think you will be glad you chose her, too.

Thank you so much for everything, Elizabeth. This community is better off because you are in it.

So if you’re looking for a good honest reliable workers comp lawyer, Elizabeth is definitely the person for you, she takes all your calls. If she busy she’ll call you back. HONESTY, he was not going to nor allow lies to enter this case and he's willing to call you out on it! -2nd. Availability, most Lawyers would talk to you initially and then hand you over to the paralegal. Mike was available even in the middle of the night!!! -3rd. Accountability, You can count on motions, filings, etc. to be done right and on time! -4th. THE LOVE, My wife and I felt the love and care from the moment we stepped through the door! On holidays Mike even remembers you and your family, rather a card written by his hand or a simple email!

Mike, I APPRECIATE ALL THE HARD WORK AND LONG..... HOURS! May GOD bless you and your family with love, good health, prosperity, and long life! AGAIN THANK YOU, AND MUCH LOVE BROTHER!!!

It is my opinion that if you have been hurt in an accident, especially one that changes your life forever, there is no other legal team you should consider than Werner Law. Our HOA's tax situation was a mess, a residual left to us from the developer. Twenty-five tax parcels, all with errors; incorrect appraisals, addresses and names. We owed tens of thousands of dollars in back taxes and liens. It was an overwhelmingly complex jumble. Attorney David Metzger and the WT team successfully negotiated with the county and the developer. Our appraisals were corrected and our tax bills were reduced from $31,000 to $31.00. David secured tax refunds that far exceeded our liens. He also negotiated a positive settlement with the developer for our HOA
There are plenty of attorney's who can assist with appraisals, that's easy. Few are the firms who understand how to work with the counties and cities property tax departments to resolve complex issues. Williams Teusink is one these select firms. Do not hesitate.

I was extremely pleased with this firm, in particular with the services of my attorney, Karen Scott. Ms. Scott is professional, courteous, and kind. If you find yourself in a similar situation, don't go it alone. Get a consultation with Karen Scott at Williams Teusink. You won't be disappointed.

We are honored to write a positive review for the real estate law firm Williams Teusink and specifically attorney David Metzger. We came to know this law firm at a critical moment in our lives. We were on the verge of losing our home property of 15 years due to a miniscule delinquent property tax amount that had not been paid for many years due to an upcharge by the county based on rising property values. As diligent property owners, we paid our taxes on a regular basis via our mortgage company but inexplicably that upcharge in taxes was missed. Unbeknownst to us, there is a policy that allows the county tax commissioner to sell off unpaid taxes to third party companies who then in turn charge substantial interest to the property owner and if unpaid can lead to the loss of our property.

However, with Williams Teusink, that did not happen because their professional prowess. We would have been at the mercy of seedy third party companies and dealing with the bureaucracy of  county governments. Real estate transactions can be complex, and it is essential to have a competent attorney to help navigate the legal process. We specifically worked with attorney David Metzger who was our voice on the ground. It was a complex case plus the fact that we are living overseas. We could not be more impressed with David's professionalism, knowledge, expertise and empathy. In fact the whole office was responsive including the associate attorney Imani Redd, the paralegal and the administrative assistant. It was a stressful set of events and their staff treated me with care and support.

From the start, they took the time to understand our needs and goals and provided us with a comprehensive plan to achieve them. They were always available to answer our questions (we were in a time zone  12 hours ahead) and provided guidance throughout the entire process. Their attention to detail and thoroughness gave us peace of mind that everything was being handled correctly.

We want to say a few things more about attorney David Metzger. He is a great communicator, keeping me informed every step of the way and ensuring that I understood all the legal aspects of the this complex transaction. He was proactive in identifying and addressing any potential issues before they became problems. Thank you David for your sage wisdom and steady demeanor.

Overall, we highly recommend the Williams Teusink law firm to anyone in need of legal representation for their real estate transactions. Their professionalism, expertise, exceptional customer service and empathy set them apart from the rest. Thank you for your excellent work!

He leads an office of caring assistants and attorneys who make sure whatever needs to be done, gets done in a timely manner. We have worked with Tristan and his office for over 4 years. Lots of emotion, frustration, and confusion. However, when I spoke with Tristan, he was able to quell the confusion and give me faith that everything would be taken care of. Our situation felt as if a rug was pulled from underneath us and I had no idea what we were going up against. However, Tristan was on the front lines with us from day one and has never backed down. We would be in a much different situation if I had not decided to work with him. If you’re looking for a law firm and attorney that will go above and beyond for you, has an expansive knowledge of law, and will go to bat for you no matter what, then look no further. You’re right where you need to be! Let Tristan and everyone at Morrison and Hughes take care of everything, they will always show up and go above and beyond for you!

Regardless of the outcome, which was a loss I would never recommend this lawfirm. As a federal government employee in need during the unorthodox gutting of the U.S. government system, and internal racial bias I needed assistance. This firm charged $1,900 to write one letter that only supported the case I put together on my behalf. That is taking advantage of the political issues at-hand on a whole nother level. Sinful.

I must say, I have never written a review about businesses on Google.  But if it had not been for the reviews on here prior to making the decision to inquire & choose a lawyer I may would have a hard time going through the disability retirement process.  I shopped for days & weeks. Went thru many consultations & phones call & emails & long nights going thru the process of elimation (pros & cons) ; I came with choosing The Vaughn Law Firm (Mr. Henson). I'm so thankful I chose to work with them.  I wouldn't tell a lie & wouldn't most likely leave a review if I want greatly satisfied.  I want my experience with them to be the same with someone who has a good case to file any federal claim, to experience exactly what I did.  The process went very smooth, with consistent communication (whether phone or email), & quick.  No one could ask for anything better in a lawyer.  I had applied for Disability retirement in my own & was denied & Mr. Henson spoke with me knowing the limited time I had with filing for an appeal & challenge the case . He didn't make any promises unlike most attorneys who just want your money & not your case or success. He was real & down to earth with letting me know he was going to do his best & keep proceeding if denied.  That's the reality we people need when dealing with businesses.  I definitely feel that if you have a good case you should proceed with The Vaughn Law Firm !!! I didn't expect to write so much but I could go on for days to share my prays.
Thank you Mr. Henson for all your help & the work you put in & accepting my case.
Shanita Weeden-Griffin

I am a lifelong federal employee, and Mr. Henson will always be my attorney until I retire.  Mr. Vaughn and Mr. Henson are very fair and honest! reviews.ownerResponse: Thanks for calling Mr. Kern. If this is the call that I believe it is, I took it myself. It involved an emergency intervention in a landlord-tenant dispute on behalf of the roommates who were tenants. I would have had to lay aside all of my current work for at least two days to address the situation, and my current caseload simply did not permit that. In addition, I had recently talked with the attorneys I know who practice in this area, and everyone is swamped and none of us are taking cases at this time. There may be others, but I knew of no one. So, I was not too busy. I just knew of no one who was available. I am sorry that you had your problem to begin with, sorry I could not help and sorry I knew of no one who could step in. I did not, however, fail to provide a referral because I was too busy. Best of luck.

reviews.text: The worse attorney I have ever come in contact with. I found him to be a liar and one of the most dishonest attorneys I have ever met in my life. He seemed to be only too happy to help my sister in forcing my mother (who had been diagnosed with cancer and was only given a few days to live)and was on strong pain killers and cancer drugs to change her will for a price. In my opinion, he would do anything for money. I think he is the most unethical person I have ever met in my life. I don't believe he knows what good morals are.
reviews.ownerResponse: I will allow the judge's ruling regarding Ms. Peterson's allegations to stand as my response. From pages 6 and 7 of the December 28, 2016, order of DeKalb County Probate Court Judge Jeryl D. Rosh, which refers to Ms. Peterson as "Movant": "21. Movant knows or should know that the Will was prepared and executed completely ethically and legally by decedent's attorney, A. Thomas Stubbs. Movant knows or should know that her statements to the contrary are false. 22. Movant knows or should know that the estate was administered properly as a legal and ethical matter by the executor ... and his counsel, A. Thomas Stubbs. Movant knows or should know that her statements to the contrary are false. 23. In contrast, the record of this proceeding is replete with documents written by Movant showing that, during the administration of this estate, she has been vindictive, disrespectful, unnecessarily litigious, has acted in bad faith, and has unnecessarily expanded these proceedings, all without justification."

Were it possible, I would assign a rating of zero stars to this experience. Regrettably, I can only describe it as truly dreadful. Throughout the entire process, updates on my case were an absolute rarity. Communication from her was limited to the initial stages and the ultimate conclusion of my case. Following the settlement, I made a request for comprehensive case-related documentation via email, but, disappointingly, I never received a response.

Her priorities appear solely focused on monetary gain, overshadowing any consideration for the well-being of the individual involved. It pains me to admit that I cannot, in good conscience, recommend her services to anyone. It is my sincere suggestion to avoid investing your time in this endeavor.

Initially, as you consider engaging her services, she presents herself as professional and highly educated. However, once the papers are signed, the dynamic drastically shifts. Communication becomes virtually non-existent, and even when you manage to reach out to her, the information provided seems designed to create the illusion of progress on your case. Astonishingly, she never initiates contact herself, and I mean "never" in the truest sense of the word. In my personal experience, my case stretched out over a span of two years.

Moreover, the places she directs you to seem to have a vested interest in collaboration with her, raising a red flag. The invoices you receive are exorbitant, appearing tailored to benefit her more than your situation. It's almost as if they view you as a source of easy money, a legal maneuver that eerily resembles a scam. Both my daughter and I were represented by her firm, albeit on separate cases, and unfortunately, we felt shortchanged by the whole ordeal. I must regrettably advise against considering her services. My encounter with her proved to be a deeply unsatisfactory one.

I implore you to continue exploring other options. Entrusting your case to her could potentially leave you with substantially less compensation than you anticipated. It appears her primary concern lies in maximizing her own financial gain, even at the expense of the compensation rightfully due to you following your accident.

The old saying ignorance of the law was never truer than in the disorderly conduct case I received in Atlanta. The arresting officer( obviously the "badd apple" police supporters reference) gave me misinformation/disinformation that I would get a signature bond and be out the in 6 hours. So imagine the disappointed surprise when A night in jail almost turned into 2 days in jail due to the system being down preventing me bonding out. That was my introduction to Georgia law( not for the faint of heart). Two individuals helped me from getting swept up by torrent of Georgia law. First the CO who assisted me accessing my money to bond out( God bless you) and my lawyer Ezekiel Tunde. He was appointed by the court but he worked as hard as if I hired him myself. He got me a plea of ascensia, fielded all my calls, patiently explained circumstances and answered all my questions. He even answered civil legal questions of totally unrelated matter. He is more than a consummate professional, He is a good human being. I would undoubtedly hire him again if I had a case in Georgia but I plan on staying far far away from that State( respectfully).

Odd thing to give a 5-star review to, but our Dekalb County Jury Service’s Team deserves it! Warm, welcoming, jovial, friendly, clearly values the time and schedules of citizens, helps citizens respect the sacred process they’re participating in... the list goes on. If you’re stressed about jury duty, don’t be: you’re treated as an honored guest in the courthouse.
Done early!

Dekalb County strives for excellence in this area and it shows. Our jury services manager (Ms. Angie?) was very good. Everything was great (comfy chairs, wifi, personable people who went out of their way to explain the process...) except the snack consignment in the basement. Skip it and go to the corner and get Chick Fil A or Waffle House. I left the courthouse actually disappointed that I wasn't picked to be on the jury.
Prices was painless and easy. They do they best to make it comfortable and fast. Cozy chairs and WiFi available.

This may sound crazy but they actually made this a pretty nice experience. You can really tell that someone at Dekalb County did a lot of work on how to make people less inconvenienced and I think it makes a huge difference with people showing up and wanting to be a part of the process. We all got notes directly from the judges involved thanking us for being part of the process and my first jury duty experience was WAY WAY better than I had anticipated. I have to think that it isn't like this everywhere which is why everyone tries to get out of it. I wanted Dekalb County to know that the effort didn't go unnoticed.

We are very satisfied with the outcome of the case. Lynda Parker is delightful to work with, and very knowledgeable regarding workman's comp. We can only give her our highest recommendation as a thank you for a job well done.

I am writing to share my experience with your firm after a recent phone call. I reached out to introduce our courier services, which are tailored to the needs of law firms. Unfortunately, the individual who answered the phone was extremely rude and unprofessional. Rather than simply informing me that your firm does not require courier services, he abruptly stated that your firm does not accept such calls and asked that I not contact you again, then hung up on me.

I found this response to be completely unnecessary and disappointing. A courteous "no thank you" would have sufficed, and the way the call was handled reflects poorly on the professionalism of your firm. It is important for me to note that such behavior heavily influences my decision to support any of your business, and based on this interaction, I would not consider recommending your services.

reviews.text: It was difficult sorting through all the different attorneys that represent bankruptcies. One thing I liked was that they get you doing the financial training course while they are getting the whole process set up. This is good to get it out of the way because when the court asks if you have done it, you have the certificate to give them. I believe if it is left up to the individual to do it later it will hold up the process all around. So from then on we felt comfortable to stay with them to help us and take the ride to the end. Worst law firm ever. I filed a bankruptcy in 2012. The courts had taken one garnishment from my check too many. I had to write the courts to get my money back. I never thought the courts had responded because I never received the money. I just let it go because I had and still have medical issues that take up my time.

I have a recent issue with my bankruptcy so I called the firm. The attorney who handled my case told me the courts had sent me the money owed me, but he kept the money because I owed the law firm. I found my receipts that showed I had paid them all but $75. I would have paid them the $75 out of the check and I still would have had $300 for me that I needed at the time. I don't know how he could cashed a check made out to me. He said he had tried to reach me but couldn't. He could have mailed me my check. I need them to help me because the bankruptcy has been reopened. All I have gotten is the run around from the attorney.

So now, I will have to try and find a new bankruptcy attorney. Please do not use this law firm if you want good representation for your case.

Marlon is the BEST. Incredibly knowledgeable when it comes to personal injury cases, he’s helped multiple friends of ours & is our go-to when it comes to anything car accident related or injury related.

From start to finish, he kept me informed and handled everything with care and expertise. I felt supported and confident throughout the entire process. Truly a top-notch attorney! ☺️

I've worked with a few attorneys in the past and he was by far the most responsive and kind.  He took time to explain what was going on and the time frames things were occurring over.  Highly recommend using Marlon for your personal injury needs!
Service was amazing and helped me and my wife save so much time and heart ache with our issue. Will definitely recommend and use again if needed!

Marlon and his associates worked hard to gather all the necessary documents,  information, and depositions to get me my best settlement.  He was completely responsive when I had questions and never failed to respond in a timely manner. If I ever needed to, I would certainly hire him again.
Best lawyer in the country, and I do not sleep on Rhine law firm .every accident I've goten into ,the first call Ive made is Rhine law firm . I've used him at least 3 times.

Before I found Marlon Rhine, another lawyer thought my case was not worth his time. Wow! Was he wrong! Marlon won far more than I expected. Not only is he a great attorney, he is a great negotiator, which makes $$$ thousands of dollars difference in what you take home. Courteous and kind too.

As an attorney, Marlon embraces the rare combination of professional and genuine in his approach to case management. I am thankful that I had him in my corner throughout a challenging and lengthy litigation.
Highly recommend. Great communication made the process easy.

Marlon is one of the most competent yet compassionate attorneys I’ve ever known. He is highly knowledgeable and always treats people with kindness and dignity. My husband and I were involved in a very serious accident when another motorist TBoned our car at high speed.  We were both seriously injured and spent weeks in the hospital.  We had never been through anything like that before and overwhelmed with our own recovery and the layers of insurance even though  the accident was not our fault as ruled by the police. Marlon Rhine did a wonderful job representing us and helping us understand the process. He gave us excellent advice and ultimately helped us both feel more in control of a terrible situation and future considerations as we continue to recover. We are incredibly grateful to him and would 100% recommend his firm !!

I am a single mother And full-time caregiver to my elderly grandmother, and I work a full-time job with limited income so I was devastated when someone pulled out in front of me and totaled my only means of transportation Marlon Rhine, was my lawyer and did a sensational job!! He got me an amazing settlement. I had never been through anything like this before therefore, I didn’t really know what to expect. he made this experience very easy for me, so what I thought was devastating set me up for a place I never expected to be he was generous. He was kind and he was there for me 24 seven I would recommend Marlon Rhine to anyone. terrific, Lawyer!!!

Marlo Rhine has set the bar high above the rest with his Exemplary Service, that marvelous service that every other firm should adopt and model them selves after. I hardly ever had to check on my case because he personally always kept me well informed throughout my entire case. The Greatest To Do It!!!

The Rhine Law Firm surpassed all my expectations in handling my MVA case. Dealing with the aftermath of an accident or personal injury is undeniably challenging, and I can remember the exact moment when I realized that I could not handle my case on my own. However, after having a conversation with Marlon, following a recommendation from a mutual friend, my worries were immediately assuaged.

Throughout the entire process, Marlon and the Rhine Law Firm eased my burdens. Marlon's level of advocacy, compassion, and transparency was unparalleled, ensuring a smooth journey throughout my entire case.

From the very beginning, I was never left confused or uninformed. Marlon went above and beyond to keep me fully engaged and informed at every step. I also greatly appreciated that he took the time to explain all the legal proceedings and jargon in the simplest terms, ensuring that I understood everything. It was truly reassuring to have such a knowledgeable and patient advocate by my side.

Additionally, what impressed me the most was how Marlon empowered me throughout the process. He genuinely valued my input and made sure my voice was heard. There is absolutely no question that Marlon has a deep passion for helping people, and this sincerity is reflected in every step that Marlon takes in his client advocacy.

Overall, the Rhine Law Firm provided an outstanding service that went above and beyond my expectations. Their dedication, expertise, and genuine care for their clients greatly sets them apart from all others. I am immensely grateful for their support, and I highly recommend them to anyone seeking legal representation.

The Rhine Law Firm has been excellent during my accident recovery. Unfortunately, I was in a second rear-end accident and knew exactly who to call. Thank you Marlon for always being so caring and for easily explaining to me all the legalities of my accident claim.

If you’re in need of a personal injury attorney in the Atlanta area, you can’t do better than Mr. Rhine. His expertise is unmatched, but what really stood out to me was his genuine compassion. He had my family’s best interest in mind during a stressful time, and I’m very grateful.
This law firm is the one to go to when you're in an accident.

Working with The Rhine Law Firm, LLC was a wonderful experience! Marlon is both extremely knowledgeable and professional. He  makes sure to thoroughly explain the process of your case and is more than willing to answer any questions that you may have. I highly recommend Marlon for any personal injury related cases!

Marlon is the best! I was involved in a car accident in November of 2019 and had severe bruising afterwards. It wasn’t pretty, but I had no breaks or tears and didn’t think it would be a high-value case. Marlon disagreed. His extensive experience has shown him how that type of injury can have deeper effects that emerge over time, and boy was he right.

Over a few months I ended up with knee issues (still present) and nerve damage. He helped me find a wonderful PT and I underwent imaging and injections. The bills still weren’t high, and insurance wasn’t even offering to pay enough to cover them. Marlon kept me constantly informed about the conversations he was having with the insurance company, and knew that if I was ok to wait and be patient, they would come around.

I understand that not everyone can afford to wait, and I was very lucky to be able to. It took just over 2 years, but the insurance company agreed to pay limits, and then some. During that time I was able to seek care and do PT and know that Marlon would see to it that I got those costs back with the settlement.

Marlon’s transparency and encouragement made me feel like I was doing the right thing the whole time, and I trusted that everything he suggested was in my best interest. I can’t even tell you how many times he told me “The important thing is getting you better.”

He cares deeply about his clients, he knows everything there is to know about the processes and the insurance carriers, and I felt extremely comfortable with his expertise.

I went into our first meeting thinking my injuries weren’t a big deal, and I’m so glad that he encouraged me to take the time to make sure I was ok before accepting the first (super low) offers. Sure enough, I needed more care than I initially expected, and got the care I needed without fear that I would go into debt for it.

I can’t recommend him highly enough, he was such a great resource and friend during a nerve-wracking time (no pun intended). Should you be unlucky enough to need a great attorney, Marlon will absolutely make the whole process painless (no pun intended again haha) and do everything he can to make sure you’re taken care of.

Gold standard for lawyers I can trust. Helped me focus on physical recovery after an accident, calmed my nerves when insurance adjusters refused to take responsibility or pay for needed treatment, and was attentive to my needs throughout. Definitely recommend - not many great lawyers like Marlon who care out there.

Marlon Rhine is an amazing personal injury attorney. If you are looking for an honest, trustworthy attorney to represent you then look no further. Marlon is passionate about helping people who have been injured. If you've been hurt due to someone else's negligence, I highly recommend that you call The Rhine Law Firm. Don't waste your time going to a "mill" law firm where you're just another number. You should be looking for an attorney that is responsive, caring, and who will give your case personal attention. Find an attorney that puts the needs of his clients above his own bottom-line. That's Marlon.

I have known Marlon Rhine for a number of years.  Marlon is a passionate legal advocate who is highly experienced in his area of law.  He works hard for those he represents and isn’t afraid to push back against insurance companies.  Not only is he a fierce advocate, Marlon is also friendly and responsive to his clients.  I would not hesitate to refer Marlon to anyone I knew who needed a plaintiff’s attorney to help with any sort of personal injury claim.

Marlon has handled several personal injury cases for friends and family of mine, and achieved excellent results. He's a superb attorney and the one you want if you or a loved one have been injured in an accident.

reviews.text:
I would strongly caution anyone considering Dawoskin Law Firm in Decatur, GA. I paid them a deposit for my case, and after only two payment stages, they decided to withdraw. On top of that, they tried to force me into a weekly payment plan, which made no sense for the situation and felt like a money trap rather than a professional arrangement.

After taking my deposit and partial payments, they pulled out instead of following through. In my opinion, that shows a complete lack of reliability and accountability. A law firm should be committed to their clients, not creating confusing payment demands and then walking away after they’ve been paid.

If you’re looking for dependable legal help, I’d recommend looking elsewhere. My experience with Dawoskin Law Firm left me with serious doubts about their professionalism and their commitment to the people they claim to represent.

I employed Richard Grossman to defend me as I had been charged with Insurance Fraud in the State of Georgia. The downside is that I live outside the state of Georgia. I had called several attorneys in the State of Georgia to handle my case and was denied by many and/or if they did accept my case, the cost to represent me was enormous. A good friend of the family recommended Mr. Grossman and when I called him, he took the time to listen to my case, offered some reasonable solutions, especially since I did not live in Georgia, and agreed to represent me at a very reasonable cost.

Once Mr. Grossman and I agreed upon the terms, he IMMEDIATELY went to work THAT DAY to file a motion that would protect me in my state of residence and prevent me from being expedited. If I had been presented with a warrant in my state of residence, this would have severely upset my home, job and family. Once everything was set-up in Georgia and the court date was scheduled, I safely traveled to Georgia to handle this legal issue, which was handled in a matter of two-weeks!!!! There was no time lost being away from my home and family and I safely traveled back to my home state knowing that this legal issue was properly handled.

Mr. Grossman has been readily available throughout the process and he kept me up-to-date on all the issues and is very knowledgeable in his field. He worked hard to represent me and make me feel comfortable, especially since this was my first time dealing with any legal problems.

Please note that I am a woman of God, and throughout this process I prayed that God would direct and guide me in the right way. I was given a bad hand due to a situation that was out of my control, but God took care of it and I believe he utilized Mr. Grossman and his expertise to work this matter through. Mr. Grossman comes highly recommended by me.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you very much I so appreciate your kindness

reviews.text: I recently had the unfortunate need for an Attorney. I was referred to Richard Grossman and put my life in his hands. Mr. Grossman's professionalism and empathy are unparalleled. His dedication and devotion to my family resulted in the best outcome one could imagine. Mr. Grossman also makes himself available to his clients no matter what the question may be. I would highly recommend Richard Grossman to anyone needing his services.

Working with Mr Rodney has truly been a blessing and an experience that exceeded every expectation. He is the type of attorney who goes above and beyond in every way possible. From the very beginning, he made it clear that we could always reach out to him for anything we needed, and he proved that time and time again. Our calls never went unanswered—if he was tied up, he always returned our calls promptly, even on his days off. That level of dedication and accessibility is rare, and it made all the difference.

What stands out most about Mr Rodney is his excellence, strong will, and compassion. He never just told us what we wanted to hear; instead, he was honest, transparent, and strategic. There were times when it was hard to fully understand his methods, but every time he walked into the courtroom, we were amazed at how his decisions worked in our favor. His confidence, intelligence, and professionalism were undeniable, and the results spoke for themselves.

It’s clear that his clients are not just “another case” or “another dollar” to him. He is truly passionate about his work and the people he represents. He carries himself with integrity, always with good intentions, and builds trust through his consistency and reliability. We never once doubted that he had our best interest at heart.

If you are looking for an attorney who is trustworthy, smart, passionate, and committed to excellence, Mr Rodney is exactly who you want on your side. His dedication, professionalism, and compassion set him apart in every way. We are beyond grateful for everything he has done, and we could not recommend him more highly! If you want a peace of mind, a lawyer with experience, good intentions, passionate I promise you , he’s your guy! He’s not that lawyer where you have to leave multiple messages with the secretary or paralegal, the amount we spent was definitely worth it.

reviews.text:
Lead Atty Jon David delegates to Shelby Mackenzie and Paul Sauls , Tim , 4 Attorneys no results

I retained this firm in October 2024 to file and pursue a federal civil lawsuit. That was the primary reason I hired them.

Between October 2024 and May 2025, I paid approximately $79,000 in legal fees and was later billed an additional $12,000, bringing the total to over $90,000. Despite that, no federal civil complaint was ever filed on my behalf.

My case was passed between multiple attorneys. I was told the complaint would be rewritten several times. I was billed for internal conferences and revisions, yet the lawsuit never left the office and was never filed in court.

The complaint drafts I received did not reflect the issues I originally brought to the firm. After months of billing and no filing, I requested a refund so I could seek new representation. My request was denied.

As a result, I am left without a filed case and without the financial ability to retain new counsel, as these funds represented my last tangible asset.

reviews.text:
I worked with Jon David at Poole Huffman on a long and draining case involving fraud by a contractor who, from day one, believed he could outsmart everyone. The facts were clear and the case itself was fairly straightforward, but the defendant, a self-proclaimed expert in contracts, litigation, and just about everything else, went to great lengths to delay, deflect, and manipulate the process. Most people in this situation would’ve walked away, absorbing the loss because litigation often costs more than the recovery. Jon was honest about that from the start. But for me, this was personal. This fraudster put my family at risk and it wasn't about the money, it was about doing what was right. I didn't want this fraudster continue to cheat others out of their hard earned money. Thankfully, I had Jon David in my corner.

From our first meeting, Jon David impressed me with his sharp grasp of the facts, command of case law, and strategic mindset. What really stood out, though, was his tenacity. He never backed down, but, as a good lawyer should do for their client, provided possible outcomes. Jon David didn’t just stay the course, he anticipated every move and was always two steps ahead.

We took the case to arbitration, where Jon David presented our case with clarity and force. The arbitrator ruled in our favor, but of course, the defendant didn’t stop there, he moved to a different state and filed for bankruptcy in what appeared to be yet another tactic to dodge responsibility. Jon David wasn’t fazed. He moved quickly to represent us in bankruptcy court and successfully proved the fraud exception. We prevailed again this time as a creditor with a non-dischargeable judgment.

This outcome was only possible because of the skill, preparation, and relentlessness of Jon David. He and his team kept me informed throughout, answered every question I had, and never once let the momentum slip. They understood how personal and frustrating this case was for me.

I can’t thank Jon David enough. His work was exceptional from start to finish. If you’re looking for a law firm and attorney that actually fights for you, and wins, I highly recommend them. You will not be disappointed.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Thank you so much for this powerful and thoughtful review. Your words mean a great deal to Jon David and the entire team at Poole Huffman. We know how personal and difficult this case was, and we’re honored to have stood beside you throughout the fight. Your trust in our team and your commitment to doing what was right were evident from day one.
We're proud to have helped achieve a just outcome and grateful for the opportunity to advocate for you every step of the way. Thank you again for allowing us to be part of this journey.

reviews.text: We just ended a federal court case in which Mr Todd Poole was our lead attorney and he definitely gets my recommendation.  Todd was fantastic in his preparation, understanding, and presentation of the facts which were instrumental in the success of our case.  Todd's clever patience allowed him to draw the most crucial facts out of the defendant.  I have to admit sitting in court watching him set the traps was quite satisfying ! Even though opposing counsel attempted to confuse the jury, Todd was able to counter every attempt.  He laid out the facts in a concise manner which made it very easy for the jury to come to a  very quick decision.  The jury came back in one hour with a verdict that was a resounding victory . . . and most importantly . .  a "just" one !  So again, I definitely recommend using Todd Poole !!!
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you so much for this incredible review and for placing your trust in Todd Poole and our team at Poole Huffman. We’re honored to have helped you navigate such an important case and are thrilled to hear that our preparation, strategy, and advocacy made a meaningful difference. We’re grateful for the opportunity to stand beside you and proud to have achieved a just outcome.

reviews.text: They have call us on their website. However, when you do call the receptionist won’t let you speak to an attorney if the damages are less than 50k. However, she only used the “they are swamped” after I told her the damages were 30k but could possibly be more. If your damages are less then 50k don’t call them. Todd and his Team have significant experience and a keen understanding of the variable complexities of litigation, carrying the bench strength, diversity, and strategic acumen to serve as both dependable collaborators and formidable adversaries.

Firstly,

I want to thank everyone at the Poole Huffman law firm for all they do. I personally worked with Tim, who has since moved on to his own company. But the whole process was extremally fast. I had issues with a certain New York based bank where they held my money for over a year. I tried reaching out to every department with no luck. So I reached out to Poole Huffman for legal assistance, and from day one Tim was on the case. He was very respectful to my... more than complex case. All in all my case was resolved in a little over a month without legal action. I truly do believe if I had never gained legal help, I would still be fighting this battle today.

Thank you again!

In my heart, I have to be honest here. Tim was my attorney who was kind enough to listen to my case for 45 mins. Most attorneys offer free consultation, so I appreciated his efforts in this manner. In a couple instances, I didn't hear from Tim in a week or more. I had to initiate contact both times asking for an update. I feel Tim did the best he could based on his ability, but I needed someone stronger to fight for me. I had ample evidence to show I did not owe any money to the other attorney. Tim charged me $2000 and told me, 'The law was not on my side', even though I had emails, invoice and appeal hearing evidence to support my claim. I feel this could have been told to me during the 45 minute conversation and other times we spoke prior to me hiring him. I was told it could cost me 80k to sue my HOA with all the evidence I have to prove my side. Perhaps the law is on my side for 80k??
Tim seems like a good dude and a Military man, so thank you for your service. I feel he didn't fight the good fight. He did get my 10k fine down to $5422.11. Add his $2000, and I only saved around $2500 when I should not have owed anything. This is how my wife and I feel, our opinions from this experience and of course there are two sides to every story.
We feel our $2000 should be refunded back to us.

reviews.ownerResponse:
Dear Mr. Anthony:
We are sorry to hear that you had a bad experience. However, we have searched our files for any reference to a "Mark Anthony" (even as a nickname) and, as far as we have been able to determine, you were never our client. We strive to provide all our clients with the very best legal representation. Even more importantly, we pride ourselves in providing accurate and objective legal advice to our clients: whether it's what they want to hear or not, they deserve to hear the good and the bad of their legal situations as we understand those situations to be. If in fact you were a client of ours and you have some name other than "Mark Anthony," please feel free to contact attorney Max Hirsh directly (max@h3-law.com). Or you can email us through our general email contact; here is a link: https://h3-law.com/contact-us/. We are more than happy to address whatever issue you may have experienced if you were a client, but if you were not a client, then obviously we cannot provide you with legal advice or representation. reviews.text: "I chose this firm based on their excellent reviews, but my personal experience was very disappointing. Not only was I not kept informed about crucial SSA appointments, but the receptionist repeatedly spoke over me, disregarding my loud tone and difficulty hearing. I have chronic pain and a TBI, which directly affects my speech and personality. I would have expected more empathy, sensitivity, and training from a law office handling disability cases. I have been trying for years to reach Attorney Flynn, but she never returned my calls. I cannot recommend this firm."
reviews.ownerResponse:
We are the third attorney for Mr. Taylor. Our disability law firm takes on challenging cases including the severely mental ill, the homeless who may be difficult to find while working on the case, and children's cases that other firms will not take. Neither Mr. Taylor nor our office were mailed an appointment notice for a physical CE appointment with a DAS contracted physician that was scheduled today.

I talked to management at Disability Adjudication Services, and she mentioned the challenges with the mail/mail room. The manager said that if the CE appointment notice is not mailed out 15 days in advance, as Mr. Taylor's was not (per the ERE it was mailed out 9 days ago), DAS will call the client which is what happened here. The receptionist was calling Mr. Taylor this week about a mental CE, when he got angry that we had not told him about the physical CE. My assistant looked in the electronic file ie ERE last Friday and told Mr. Taylor about the physical CE appointment, but he did not recall this or misunderstood due to his TBI. I had Mr. Taylor in my office yesterday going over the CE exams, calling the Adjudicator to request a zoom for a mental CE exam scheduled in Suwannee and going over his file.

Today, I called Mr. Taylor to see how the physical CE went, but he was unable to get out of bed due to pain. I conferenced him in to the Adjudicator, and he left a message as to why he missed the physical CE exam. I told him that DAS may deny him for missing an exam as they have done that in the past to clients who missed exams. Myself and another assistant spent 30 minutes on the phone as he related frustrations about how his record should be enough to win. He said we won't listen to him, but have other calls. One day, 3 of us spent @ 2 hours talking with him. We have to help other clients.

We submitted @ 27 pieces of evidence for his case. Two of Mr. Taylor's providers never sent us records, in response to our requests, so we reached out to them again and notified the Adjudicator. I tell everyone that anyone under age 50 is a more challenging case that some attorneys won't take. The best way to prevail is with mental limitations, hand limitations or difficulty concentrating due to pain/mental issues.

By the way, I return calls but was never told to call Mr. Taylor. Two attorneys have spoken to Mr. Taylor including myself in the past. Due to his irate interaction with her, the receptionist did not know how to handle him. He has been irate with all of us including me today.

Mr. Shelley:
You came to the Law Office of Kathleen M Flynn, LLC for our social security disability law firm to file a request for reconsideration. Sadly, the Adjudicator did not get all of your medical records.Your case is pending at the Social Security Administration's Disability Adjudication Services (DAS) where they have been understaffed since the Covid-19 pandemic. Your treating sources have been supportive in completing your disability paperwork. The reconsideration level in GA has traditionally had low approval rates around the state of GA of less than 20 percent. I hope you prevail at this level and do not need to go to a hearing.

Thank you for giving us a five star review. Your social security disability case was unusually complicated. We met when you came to our disability law firm, and we filed a DIB and SSI application as you reported filing years ago. After our disability assistant filed these applications, the Social Security Field office told us that you required a hearing appeal that my disability assistant filed for a hearing at the Office of Hearing Operations (OHO) Atlanta downtown office. The Social Security Field Office only forwarded the DIB, also called SSDI application, to OHO. So, our disability lawyer had to request that the SSI application be sent to OHO for your disability hearing. Despite your treating sources not completing our disability paperwork, you were awarded a fully favorable decision by the Administrative Law Judge. Erica Demsey, our associate attorney represented you.

reviews.ownerResponse: Ms. Barnett: It was a pleasure helping you and your daughter with her SSI case. Many of our colleagues do not want to represent children as there are offsets to the SSI. For example, the maximum amount of SSI allowed is $967 a month in 2025. There are offsets for child support or if a parent makes within an income range (sometimes ineligible due to the income) or if the parent and child are staying with grandparents as examples. Child SSI cases also require a lot more documentation with medical records from treating sources, teacher's questionnaires, and, depending on the type of case, psychological evaluations. Brynne Holt, attorney at the Law Office of Kathleen M Flynn, LLC represented you at the social security disability hearing at the Office of Hearing Operations (OHO) in Atlanta. You did have one of the more difficult Administrative Law Judges (ALJs) in this office. Our disability lawyers and staff are happy to know that your daughter is getting more assistance due to the financial and medical insurance provided by the Social Security Administration.

reviews.ownerResponse: Ms. Wiley: Your legal journey has been a challenging one with the Law Office of Kathleen M Flynn. You drew a very difficult Administrative Law Judge (ALJ) from the Atlanta downtown Office of Hearings Operations (OHO) who gave you an unfavorable decision. You were homeless at the time. Several Federal Court attorneys turned down your appeal to US District Court. But, like our disability attorneys and staff, you do not give up. Our social security disability assistant filed a new SSI application. After you were denied at the initial and reconsideration levels at Disability Adjudication Services (DAS), our social security disability legal assistant filed a Request for Hearing. You drew another difficult ALJ at the Atlanta downtown OHO who gave you a partially favorable decision. We appealed it to the Office of Appellate Operations (OAO) and then a Federal Court attorney took your case to US District Court. Due to errors in the ALJ decision, the case was remanded back to OHO. A new ALJ at the Atlanta downtown office granted you a partially favorable decision going back further ie 5 months after your alleged onset date ie date that you said you became disabled. With SSI applications, you cannot be granted benefits before the date of the SSI application. You were represented by disability lawyer, Erica Dempsey. You finally got the financial help you needed. No further appeals at your request.

reviews.ownerResponse:
It is always our goal to get the most money for our clients when they have a personal injury claim. We would never suggest that anyone accepts a settlement that they are not comfortable with. In fact, we have filed hundreds of lawsuits on behalf of our clients when it was determined that the settlement offered by the insurance company was insufficient.
We always confirm in writing all of our client’s medical providers. If we represented you, we certainly would have requested all of the pertinent information regarding your case from all of the doctors who treated you for your personal injury. If you feel there is a discrepancy you are welcome to request a copy of your case file for confirmation.

Seeking legal help can change your destiny.

Dear Manji Law team, and especially Attorney Matt and Ms. Nancy:

I want to express, from the bottom of my heart, my deepest gratitude for everything you did for me. I was detained at the Stewart Detention Center — a difficult place, where every day felt endless. But thanks to you, that time was short. You acted with speed, professionalism, and a commitment I will never forget.

To you, Attorney Matt, I offer a very special thank you. Thank you for expediting my case, for moving so efficiently, and for making my release on bond happen so quickly. I am convinced that without your involvement, everything would have taken much longer, and the process would have been harder and more uncertain.

You didn’t just represent me legally — you treated me like a human being. You gave me hope when I needed it most. Today, I am free, near my family, rebuilding my life — and all of that is possible thanks to your tireless work.

This magazine is a small tribute to you, for doing so much for those of us who need it the most. Thank you for giving me back my freedom, my peace of mind, and the chance to move forward.

With gratitude,
Neydi Meraz

This magazine was created from the heart, because freedom, once regained, is celebrated with eternal gratitude.

Thank you for your trust and for your heartfelt recommendation!

reviews.text: I absolutely do not recommend them. Don't waste your money. $750 for a so-called case evaluation. Any lawyer will tell you from the first appointment that they do this to rob you blind. You accept out of desperation to help your family member, and they take advantage of your situation. If you want to lose your money, hire them.
reviews.ownerResponse: Hello Marissa. We're sorry your experience turned out the way it did. Our consultation fee is $150, as you can see on our website. The $750 fee is for an in-person consultation at the Stewart Detention Center. This fee includes the attorney's travel, their efforts to schedule an appointment with Stewart staff, time spent in security, time waiting for officers to pick up the client, the one-hour consultation, and a follow-up appointment with the client's chosen contact. These services last approximately four hours and require more time than a traditional consultation. Our fees are based solely on the time involved, which you should have been informed of at the time. We apologize if we didn't explain this clearly or if you felt confused, and we hope you find an attorney who fits your needs. Thank you for your comments!

reviews.text: I had an excellent experience working with Mark Stephens after my car accident. He was professional, knowledgeable, and always kept me in the loop. Attorney Mark Stevens was supportive, emotionally, mentally and spiritually. He went above and beyond offering me advice. Getting me therapy the very next day and the proper referrals for treatments. My Surgeon said I have a very smart attorney! That made all the difference. Attorney Mark Stephens kept me grounded when I had no one else to lean on. He stayed in contact with me at least three times a week, EVERY week up to a year. With his support and help I got back on my feet. He is a kind, humble and smart attorney. reviews.ownerResponse: We are regretful that you FEEL the way you do about your experience with our firm. We recovered a fetching policy limit settlement for your case and answered a plethora of calls from you faithfully and respectfully. Furthermore, your case took longer to settle because of its complexities. After we settled your case, we sent you an itemized statement about the charges for you to review and you found an error with the med pay billing. Thankfully, you brought it to our attention and by the close of the next business day, we apologized to you, corrected the mistake and sent you a corrected statement. We stand behind our transparent process that are in place to ensure that our clients get all the recovered money that they are entitled to. Wishing you good luck with your future endeavors!

reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you for your feedback. I apologize for the misunderstanding with our staff. Georgia has some unusual jurisdictions that refuse to negotiate with lawyers regarding traffic tickets. In these areas, we inform prospective clients about this situation, as we believe it is unethical to accept a fee for a case when we are aware that we cannot improve the client’s legal circumstances. That said, it sounds like we could have exhibited more professionalism than we did.

In my personal experience, this law firm is one of the worst choices you could make. They take your money and then disappear. They claim to help low-income families, but in my opinion, that’s just a sales pitch to pull people in. Your response contains several inaccuracies and misrepresents our experience.
There were no bond violations related to the original case. That matter was already resolved, and the only remaining requirement was completion of court-ordered hours, which are currently being completed.
The bond hearing itself raised serious concerns. Comments made by the judge were delivered in a joking manner before any preliminary hearing occurred, which contributed to our dissatisfaction with how the matter was handled.
While we appreciate the appointed attorney who was assigned and who made genuine efforts to assist us—including attempting to move forward while payment arrangements were being discussed—the overall communication and handling by your office was not satisfactory.
Regarding payment, we did not refuse to pay. We attempted to make arrangements in good faith. When the remaining balance could not immediately be met, we were not allowed to continue under any modified arrangement, and the funds already paid were/would be forfeited. That was our experience.
No one informed your office that you were prohibited from communicating with me. In fact, you personally stated that you were ending communication with the mother. That claim is inaccurate.
Client satisfaction is subjective. We were not satisfied with the firm’s services overall, and this review reflects our honest experience. We stand by it.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Ms. Cook - We were surprised to see this review, as it contains statements that do not accurately reflect our firm or the services provided. Our results speak for themselves. We are among the best in Georgia, and we have received countless awards for our defense. We remain in good standing with professional organizations and the community.
1) You were not a client of our firm, and no attorney–client relationship existed between you and our office. Your boyfriend's mother retained our office for one service only – a murder case bond. The client and her were both thankful. You started contacting our office weeks after they hired us and wanted info; we were informed not to speak with you.
2) Our legal team dedicated significant time and effort to your boyfriend’s murder case bond hearing, which is why you expressed interest in retaining our firm for continued representation after the bond was completed. But, you could not pay.
3) Our firm was retained for “bond only” services, and that contractual obligation was fully fulfilled! While additional legal services were discussed, no further representation occurred because payment for those services were never received by you, despite multiple payment options, calls about money, and attempts to help you, that were presented. You could not pay.
4) In addition, to assist you all, our office covered $1,000.00 in investigative services at our own expense for the bond, which were never billed for. That included: preliminary reports, going to the murder scene, speaking to witnesses, recorded statements, gathering cameras, receipts, police reports, warrant searches, taking crime scene photos, jail visits, arguing his bond, and more.
5) Our attorney–client relationship was exclusively with the client, and legally we cannot talk to you. The law is clear: Lawyers cannot share details on cases with third parties and your reason for wanting us to share ("because you are pregnant with his child and want him home") did not permit us to violate attorney–client confidentiality or professional obligations.
6) Our firm does not provide services without compensation. We fulfilled the contract for handling bond and went up and beyond. Your boyfriend has several charges, violations of bond, and more. Your review contains statements that are not true – we cannot “take money” as you described, for additional services that you never even paid. Excuses do not equal payments. We never met you in person and we do not work for free.
We wish the best outcome for you and your family.

reviews.text:
BEWARE—STAY FAR AWAY IF YOU ACTUALLY NEED HELP!

This law firm is nothing but a money grab. I hired Shequel, yet I never even received proper representation. She had no idea what was happening in my case, and it’s clear that if your case isn’t high-profile, it isn’t a priority.

Her assistant even admitted, “It’s like if you hire a mechanic and he’s too busy, he’s just going to hand you off to another mechanic.”

Later, Shequel called me for what I assume was a consultation, informing me that her fee had increased to $4,000. Then, as if trying to be generous, she said she’d take off $500 because of my previous bad experience. After talking for another five minutes, she dropped another $500—bringing it right back to the original $3,000 I had already paid for a case she never helped me with in the first place.

This law firm is a scam. Don’t waste your time or money.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Ms. D - Your review is not truthful. We did not scam you. I would caution you not to use words that defame characters. This is a violation of the law. You paid for a service and got the fight of a lifetime! Attorney Ross had several cases at the time you asked for our representation. She still walked you through the process, spoke with you several times, talked to the courts, and when she was called in to an urgent trial she called you from the airport and missed her flight just to ensure you would be okay. She placed you with another attorney and you never paid more - which you agreed to, because we knew Attorney Ross would be in trial for weeks. We gave you several options, but we found it hard to please you. We tried to make you happy, but realize some can never be fulfilled or satisfied.

We are shocked, that even with our hard work, you are not happy. The attorney who represented you stated that you complained and gave them a hard time. It is unfortunate that our fight to keep you out of jail and get good results was not enough. We cannot please everyone, especially those who do not take accountability, want everything on their own time, those who do not follow court rules and get violated by probation, those who are not truthful about their case, and those who are not grateful. It will not change who we are - top tier attorneys seeking powerful results for our clients. Our many positive reviews speak loud and clear.

reviews.text: On Friday, March 17, 2023 my baby son turned 20 years old and that same day we received a phone call that he was incarcerated at Fannin County jail. We never heard of this county but it was known as a great tourist location with cabins. We visited our son Sunday March 19, 2023 we found out this is a small county that the majority of the population is Caucasian, we knew we had to find a lawyer that was about their business. My daughter google some lawyers in Georgia and she was determined for us to call She Law Firm. On our way home from the visit we phone Attorney Ross and she answered the phone in the middle of the day on a Sunday. We did the consultation over the phone and fell in love with her, she made us feel like we were talking to a sister/aunt about our son. She informed us up front that Fannin County is not a county for blacks but she was willing to accept the case for the fight. Three months into all of this me and my husband started to fell uneasy about our decision because we felt like nothing was being done, denied bonds, no communication, no updates, and money was getting tight. Being parents that has never had to deal with lawyers or jail for no reason not even traffic violation we were frustrated and wanted answers. After our conversation with Attorney Ross, she made us aware that even though we don't see/hear anything her and her staff were working trying to get this county to communicate with them. In August we went from denied bond and no court date to court date, bond hearing, and trial all in a 2 week time frame. We started to get nervous because everything started to move so fast so we continued to  pray for the best but was planning for the worst. Our son started to sound drained and uncertain on the phone and said he didn't know if we needed to come to court August 7, 2023 but he would call us that morning of court. No call from him or Attorney Ross so my nerves and anxiety level was all over the place, after praying and praising my Lord God, the phone rung and Attorney Ross was calling to update us about the outcome of the case. She explained to me that before court she had a crisis, her car stop working so she had to leave it on the side of the road, bum a ride from a complete stranger, hour and half late for court, and was told they were going to reschedule court since she was so late. She didn't  back down to the room full of testosterone and they decided to have court after all. After 20 minutes of talking to Attorney Ross, someone from Blue Ridge jail was calling I wanted to hang up on her but I asked if I could put her on hold and she said No I want to hear what they are saying. I merged the call and the bailiff was calling to let me know that my baby was being released. This my story my encounter with Attorney Ross and I wouldn't change anything about it. We never know why things happened but I can truly say the prolong court dates and denied bond hearing was all in the plan of the most high. My son needed this time to slow him down and to get closer to God. I would like to thank Attorney Ross and her staff for their compassion, patience, loyalty, and their hard work. Attorney Ross is like a quiet storm the wind will blow just enough to where you can see leaves move, the sky will get dark, wind start blowing so hard that trees are leaning over, stuff blowing everywhere, and then it's calm again. After the storm my baby was standing on the steps of the jail at 9pm waiting on us to come from Alabama, 10 charges dismissed, probation, no jail time, community service with restitution and fees. Thank God along with Attorney Ross my baby is home and I was given the best 21st Wedding Anniversary, after she fought so hard she decided to bless us one more time and we will forever be grateful. I would recommend her to anyone that is in need of a compassionate attorney. One thing for certain she is a child of God and it shows regardless how you may come at her she will continue to stay professional BUT she will give you a nice/nasty response and continue to fight for her client.
reviews.ownerResponse: Wow! Such a powerful, intentional review. This review shows how hard we worked. Attorney Ross and team traveled hours to this small little county, worked late nights with the investigator, and fought until the end. When the results were mentioned, even other lawyers were surprised that the judge granted our request. We won! Thanks for your trust.

I Highly Recommend Carlton Law, LLC!

If you are dealing with an overzealous HOA Board that is quick to escalate matters into litigation, I strongly recommend Carlton Law, LLC.

My HOA Board filed a civil lawsuit against me after I submitted a letter—on behalf of myself and several other homeowners—objecting to a proposed policy. It was a stressful and overwhelming situation, and I immediately began searching for legal help.

After calling several law firms, I contacted Matthew W. Carlton—and to my surprise, he answered the phone himself. He took the time to listen and promptly scheduled a free consultation for the very next day.

During our meeting, Mr. Carlton quickly assessed the situation, clearly explained my options, and recommended a strategic course of action. He then executed that plan with professionalism, skill, and a strong understanding of HOA law. His insight, responsiveness, and sound judgment made a difficult situation much easier to navigate.

Thanks to his foresight and effective approach, the lawsuit was ultimately dismissed.

Before taking any action involving your HOA or Board of Directors, I highly recommend consulting with Matthew W. Carlton. His guidance took a tremendous amount of stress off my shoulders, and I am truly grateful for his outstanding service.
Mr. Carlton is extremely knowledgeable and professional. We would not hesitate to re-hire him in the future, if need be.

For fifteen years, I have known Matthew Carlton as a devoted father, a loyal husband, and a generous neighbor. Recently, I discovered that a considerable sum had been fraudently withdrawn from my bank account. When I was unable to get the bank to correct the situation, I employed Mr. Carlton. He very quickly resolved the dispute entirely in my favor.

Needed an LLC for a rental property in GA, I live in FL.  Easy to enough do on my own, but on advice from a friend that owns property, I found a "brick and mortar attorney in the state the property is located", and proceeded.

He said he has done plenty of these.

The first issue was I didnt hear back and assumed all was well after I got him the information needed to accurately create the LLC.  About 2 months in I called to confirm everything was done, and he told me he hadnt even filed the paperwork.  He seemed apologetic, but he basically took my money and forgot about the job.  Of course he needed more info to file, which I provided immediately.

Long story short, I paid about 500 bucks total in fees to him, and the state apparently, for 3 attempts to register my business.

Finally got it registered on the third attempt.

Apparently he didn't do a search on the GA commerce site to see if the business was already listed, that cost me, and then each time he filed, he said the state requires another $125 fee.

Thats not true according to the state of GA.  One fee to file within a reasonable timeframe, even if the application requires revision or correction.

I hate being lied to.  Just tell me you want more money to file the paperwork, dont tell me the state requires another fee each time.

Whatever.  It's done.  Small price to pay to have the peace of mind.

Fast forward a year and go to sell the house.  Let the realtor know the property is in an LLC.  Completely wasted her time as she struggled with due diligence, and cant find any evidence that I am not the homeowner.  No legal evidence of an LLC tied to that rental property!

Essentially, he created the LLC, but never filed the required documents with the state to have the property change ownership to the LLC.  The very reason for this whole process.

Initially I felt sorry for him, just thought he was an idiot.

Now reading some of these reviews it would appear that he is deceiving clients, or at a minimum takes money for services NOT rendered.  I am not certain if his intent is malicious or not, but my experience was definitely poor.

Excellent attorney. He represented me in a traffic case in Dekalb County flawlessly. He ran circles around the prosecutor. Highly recommended. Very professional with great skills. He will aggressively defend you if you need representation.

I cannot say enough good things about him. He saved me from financial ruin in a HOA situation that was completely out of my control, and for over two years fought on my behalf to make sure I received the outcome I deserved. He answered every question I had, and was always responsive with emails and phone calls. I will forever be indebted to Matthew Carlton and thankful for his skills and integrity as an attorney.

This attorney never allowed me to bring up the police report. He didn’t even subpoena the police officer to court. The officer even had body cam footage that there was not any reports of assault. I called the police to ask the landlord to stop coming to my door and banging on the door. A week later the landlord said I hit her. Yet she never told the officer that same day I called the police. I had to move within 15 minutes based on a false TPO accusation. I could have represented myself. The judge even said he was confused. Run away from this lawyer. He doesn’t know what he is doing. I would have won the case defending myself. Run and take my advice. Contact me I’ll send you proof of my claims. He lost an easy case. He has a very weird way of seeing things. He doesn’t agree with the judge on 3 different times I had to appeal the case . He lost. I had already gave him several thousands of dollars. RUN

Mr. Carlton is a knowledgeable, professional attorney. Highly recommend working with him.

Mr Carlton knows the courts inside and out, and leverages that deep knowledge to achieve outstanding results. He is exceptionally smart and articulates arguments persuasively. He is one of the top lawyers in Decatur without a doubt. Highly recommend.

Mathew Carlton is a wonderful lawyer. I hired him to represent me in a judgment case against my HOA. The law firm that represents my HOA has a reputation for being unethical. I hired Mathew because of his extensive experience and ability to navigate the law in favor of his clients against such firms. I'd recommend Mathew to anyone seeking experience, professionalism, a fair price, and results.
