Mr. Carlton is a respected attorney who can be trusted to handle difficult matters.  If you are wondering whether you should hire him, the answer is yes.

Very knowledgable and hardworking attorney. I've known Mr. Carlton professionally for over 3 years and he has always been very helpful if I needed legal advice. He works very hard for his clients and is not afraid to go the extra mile in court.

Matthew Carlton is highly skilled attorney conveniently located in Downtown Decatur.  Beside for being knowledgeable and experienced, I have always found him to be understanding and easy to talk with. I highly recommend him.

Mr. Carlton stated that he previously represented HOAs and now represents homeowners against HOAs.  I was referred to Mr. Carlton from a partner in another firm who said he’d fight for me. That partner, and two other partners in different firms all stated I had a good case. Not only did Mr. Carlton not fight for me, he went back to the HOA. Highlights:

I emailed Mr. Carlton a witness list for him to get started on preferably before writing the complaint, and have affidavits prepared for filing with the complaint. Mr. Carlton did not speak to any witnesses.
I gave Mr. Carlton several documents I had received from the HOA. Either he never reviewed them or lacks the attention to remember anything he reviewed.
I had to spend 6 hours revising the beginning of the complaint – all he had to do was write the causes of action/Counts and he couldn’t even do that. And nearly a year later, Mr. Carlton asked ME to review and revise/mark up the complaint for him as an amended Complaint.
Two Counts of my complaint were completely false. I never told Mr. Carlton what he wrote and there were no documents stating that. He added those on his own accord.
Mr. Carlton never wrote a cause of action/Count regarding harassment or my being stalked.

No Request for an Order requiring the HOA Board to honor their decision in the relief section. Instead, it was part of “Such other and further relief as deemed just and proper.” Hidden away, he never thought of this again.

I emailed Mr. Carlton a witness list for him to get started on preferably before writing the complaint, and have affidavits prepared for filing with the complaint. Mr. Carlton did not speak to any witnesses, which was crucial to my case.

Despite wanting three defendants, he stressed the HOA only should be sued.  He ended up being wrong.'

Deposition of HOA Board President.  I prepared seven pages of questions, which were a lot of yes/no regarding Board Members, two other parties. I also authorized Mr. Carlton to make it a day and half or two-day deposition. He spent 1.90 hours and only handful of questions. Because Mr. Carlton did not talk to my witnesses and refreshed his memory with documents-exhibits of the complaint he had in his possession, he failed to challenge the HOA President’s answers.

In reading the transcript, and emails from Mr. Carlton, it appears as if the HOA Board was Mr. Carlton’s client, not me. He seemed to fawn over the HOA President as such a truthful witness. I was shocked.

It was Mr. Carlton who asked the HOA President about HIS two incorrect causes of action, which then opened the door to opposing counsel accusing me of lying in my papers at my own deposition.

Mr. Carlton never prepared me for my own deposition. We could have gone over the HOA President’s deposition transcript and submitted further exhibits and testimony to counter the HOA President’s untruths.
At the end of my deposition, Mr. Carlton did not ask me one question to dispute the HOA President’s testimony or clarify answers for the record at my own deposition. It was like he wasn’t even present.

He thought my deposition was “fine.” I said it was a disaster and said opposing counsel would probably file a dismissal. He disagreed. I was right on both.

By the end, he completely and utterly lost site of the reason for my wanting to sue the HOA Board. Mr. Carlton caved to the HOA’s attorney and especially the HOA President. I knew then Mr. Carlton really never had a grasp of what my case was about.

I am not saying I would have prevailed at trial, but I feel my chances would have been better if Mr. Carlton had focused on and talked to my witnesses, focused more on the actions of the HOA Board and two parties in the causes of action and as stated in my deposition questions, and if he had prepared me for my own deposition. A good settlement might have been reached. In the end, I spent around $8,000 and got nothing. He didn’t even try, nor even think to negotiate on my behalf.

Hope this helps.
I have worked with Mr. Carlton many times over the last 10 years. He is a creative thinker and problem solver. Highly recommend!
Matt Carlton is a rare go-to person that you want  on your side when you need someone the most. Highly recommend.

Grace Starling at Radford Scott LLP is a highly skilled and dedicated attorney. She was referred to me through a friend of a friend, and from our very first conversation she demonstrated professionalism, attentiveness, and genuine care for her clients.

I am extremely grateful for Grace’s dedication, communication, and support, and I would wholeheartedly recommend her to anyone seeking an attorney who is highly competent, empathetic, and truly committed to their clients.

I can’t say enough about the outstanding experience I had with Grace A. Starling and the entire team at Radford Scott LLP Law Firm. From the moment I reached out to them regarding my employment termination case, they treated me with respect, compassion, and constant professionalism.

Thanks to her relentless efforts and the support of her incredible team, we won a substantial settlement that not only validated my experience but also gave me the fresh start I desperately needed. I finally felt heard, and I got the justice I deserved.

If you’re facing an employment termination that you feel is unjust or any kind of employment dispute, do not hesitate—call Grace Starling and her firm immediately. They are the best in the business, and they truly care about their clients. I’m forever grateful!

What I appreciate most about him is his balanced approach—he’s confident in his expertise but never overly optimistic. He tells you the truth, not just what you want to hear, which I really respect. I’m also very selective about who I work with and trust with my family’s interests (I actually let go of the first attorney I hired at another firm), but Justin absolutely earned that trust. I hired this firm to handle a wrongful termination case, and it was a complete disaster. They initially assured me they would fight for my rights, but after taking my case, they went silent. I rarely received updates, and when I asked about the progress, I got vague answers and no real plan of action.

After more than a year of inaction, they suddenly dropped my case, claiming they couldn’t represent me anymore—right when deadlines were approaching. Their lack of professionalism and communication wasted valuable time and put my entire case in jeopardy.

This firm is unreliable, inattentive, and does not prioritize their clients. If you need an employment lawyer, look elsewhere."

This is the most unprofessional law firm I’ve ever dealt with. I hired Jake Knanishu in August 2023 for an EEOC discrimination case, and he wasted over a year and half of time with poor communication, no clear plan, and constant delays. At the most critical moment—when the EEOC finally responded—this firm dropped my case, leaving me with little time to find new representation and risking the statute of limitations.

This firm does not fight for their clients; they waste your time and abandon you when it matters most. Avoid at all costs."

Justin Scott is such a thorough and phenomenal attorney. His most admirable characteristics, in my opinion, are authenticity and integrity. On top of that, he will leave no stone unturned when it comes to your case. I’ve had the pleasure of watching him be great for nearly 4 years. I rarely post reviews, but I was compelled to share in case someone is in need of top-notch employment attorney. I highly recommend this company.

It was almost three years ago that Mr. Jake Knanishu started a case for me regarding a P.U.A. claim with the Georgia Department of Labor. After my claim was turned down, I was looking for a law firm that could help me. Mr. Knanishu appealed the case with the result that the case was confirmed as eligible. After confirmation of eligibility, I was completely ignored by the Department of Labor for almost two more years. No response from them, even my e-mails were not answered anymore. I got in touch with the County Commissioner, the Commissioner of the Department of Labor, and the Department of Labor itself. There were no results, not even an e-mail from any of the above parties involved. Mr. Knanishu found a source in the Georgia Attorney General’s office who finally made it possible to find a way to locate the money. I got compensated without going to court. Mr. Knanishu saved me thousands of dollars of additional expenses. This is unheard of, and I am sure that no other law firm or lawyer would have done the same thing. Mr. Knanishu is highly recommended. Not just his professional manners impressed me but also his dedication to the case. A nightmare came to an end due to his professional help, kindness, and dedication to a client.

I spoke with Grace Starling at this practice about a contract dispute and found her to be a good listener who explained the legal components of my claim to me in lay terms and provided insightful guidance. Her rate is also very reasonable.

We hired Grace and she was absolutely amazing. She had so much empathy and patience with our case. It wasn’t the easiest case to win but she fought hard at every stage, while keeping us well informed. We never got out of touch with Grace and she always returned my calls immediately. I am so so thankful to her and will definitely recommend her to anyone.

They lowballed me tremendously all I could do is cry. I had to have My Attorney tell me that it was a good offer. It didn’t even cover all of my medical bills. I have emailed My Attorney several times called the office several times. Everyone is ignoring me now I just feel really heartbroken. I lost a lot went through a lot of pain and stress physically and mentally after this accident and I just feel like this Office doesn’t care or have any type of sympathy to what I have been through. I hope you never need them, but call them if you do!

In 2023, two days before Christmas, while cycling, I crashed into a car that pulled out of a parking lot in front of me. I ended up going to the ER for fours and spending several weeks in physical therapy. Thankfully, I had no broken bones and no long-term injuries.

My wife, who was out of town during the accident, called one of my friends to check on me. One of the things he told me is that I needed to get a lawyer. And he sent me the contact info for Hagen Rosskopf LLC.

The staff from Hagen Rosskopf was very attentive to my case and transparent about the process. They walked me through every step, answered every question and got me good results. I didn’t pay a penny for my medical care and the settlement paid for a replacement bike and equipment, with money left over for pain and suffering.

We never want to need an injury attorney, but if you need one, call the staff of Hagen Rosskopf. They will take great care of you!

reviews.text: they use scare tactics be aware
reviews.ownerResponse: Who are you? Who did you speak to and what was the scare tactic employed?  The only scare tactic I ever use that I am aware of at the firm is if someone breaches their contract and I threaten to come off the case.  I may have employed a scare tactic, but judging by your name real reviews you are likely not a client or someone we have ever encountered instead you are trying to create importance in the review community.  If you email me what happened, then I want to know who did this and how they used a "scare tactic."  The reason is as follows I like to learn what I am doing that maybe wrong so I can fix the problem.  If you never interacted with this firm then take the review down please.  It hurts people's businesses when people put up reviews without explaining the issue.  If you decide not to reveal yourself I will mark you in the individuals' category of trolling reviewers who are not lawyers.  Scare tactics and an honest assessment of the facts are not one and the same but how we choose to perceive them can make us believe one or the other.  If you want to reveal who you are call my office or send an email.  If you don't I will move to have this taken down as spam.

reviews.text:
I am incredibly grateful to Allah for blessing me and my family with Attorney India Ali and her law firm! They taught me the critical importance of having a Trust instead of relying solely on a Will. While a Will can be contested, a Trust is legally binding and final, providing greater security and peace of mind.

As Muslims, we know that none of our material possessions or wealth will follow us into the Hereafter. However, it is our responsibility to ensure that we leave this world without causing unnecessary hardship or disputes among our loved ones. Having your affairs in order can relieve your immediate family—spouses, children, and others—of the stress and challenges that often arise after a loved one’s passing.

It is vital to address debts before we die to avoid leaving a financial burden on our families. At the very least, it’s important to have plans in place to ensure any outstanding debts are covered. Additionally, a Trust allows you to make decisions about your afterlife—such as how and where you wish to be buried or whether you want to be placed on life support. These are deeply personal matters that should be decided while you’re alive, sparing your family from the burden and potential conflict of making these decisions for you.

I wholeheartedly recommend Attorney India Ali Law Firm for helping you secure your wishes and protect your loved ones from unnecessary hardship. A Trust is an act of foresight and love that brings unity and peace, both in this world and beyond.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you so much for sharing your experience with Attorney Ali and our team. We are grateful for your words and your detailed reminder of the importance to plan in advance in order to protect and secure your family and intentions.

Then, was thanked for doing the phone consultation which never happened. Nothing more disappointing than having your time wasted. If you have assistants that work for you, they should know the legal matters which you do and don’t handle. It also would have been nice to have the appointment cancelled prior to. reviews.text: Jim Michael really knows his way around the legal system. He’s an outstanding attorney!!!!👍🏽

Jim Michael is simply put - the best lawyer in Georgia.
I had the pleasure to be represented by Jim twice, and both times, Jim was able to have my case fully dismissed.
Jim is extremely professional and very dedicated to get the best outcome possible for you. He was very rigorous and worked extremely hard. He was also very courteous and proceeded with such immense precision.
Jim is also very well connected with numerous prosecutors and judges all over the state of GA, making him the best lawyer to negotiate and present a deal that would best suit the outcome desired.
I strongly recommend Jim to anyone and everyone whom is looking for a formidable lawyer to represent you.
A job exceptionally well done.

Attorney Michael was precise, communicative and a true advocate and he represented our family with the utmost integrity during our crisis. I highly recommend Attorney Jim Michael to represent your interests before the Judicial System.

I decided he was the right person for my case which were difficult one. Jim handled my case with professionalism and got it dismissed. He is a smart lawyer and is a man of his word. I would strongly recommend  him , if  you have a difficult case please consider him. Thanks Jim I will be forever grateful.

Very grateful to Mr. Michael for helping us navigate the court process for a traffic violation. We had a very favorable outcome thanks to his ability to prepare and negotiate with the court solicitors. We would not have been able to do it on our own.
Wonderful experience. Great customer service highly recommend ❤️

We had a very difficult issue with respect to a family member that had long term potential impact if we did not get a positive outcome. We were familiar with Jim Michael from another matter he had handled for a non-family member. Based on that, we decided to engage with Jim.

Jim was awesome, providing us with a clear understanding of how we will navigate the issue, potential outcomes with a realistic outlook, and mentoring for our family member. In the end, after following Jim's advice all the way through, and Jim's clear knowledge and understanding of not only the law, but also of the judicial system, judge, clerk, etc., we had an excellent outcome. Now our goal is to never speak to Jim again! :-)

A big thanks to Jim and his help.
Very responsive and effective representation.
Mr.Michaels is a hard fighting working attorney.. He will try his hardest to get you fair justice.. I'll give him a WELL RECOMMENDATION ....

I hired Jim Michael as I was referred to him. I hired him for two separate issues in 2015 and 2017  and he did an amazing job. I was represented better than I could have ever imagined.My opinions were valued and my doubts were always reassured. My nerves were calmed and the process was always explained. Jim fought hard for me. He went above and beyond and did everything he could to ensure a great outcome.His office was highly professional and responsive. As a person of colored I never experience racism  from him. I would highly recommend  Jim to anyone seeking legal representation that is fair, honest, knowledgeable,  and will keep your best interest and unique needs at the forefront. Should any situation arise, I would not hesitate to seek representation from Jim Michael again. Thanks again!!
Attorney Jim Michael did an amazing job for our Family. He successfully guided us through some very dark, drug related times, involving one of our children. Grateful!

Do yourself a favor and stay away from this lawyer. Unless you want to go to prison for a long time !!!

Everything Mr. Michael responded to this post is true. He just chosen to leave out the part where he got mad because I wrote the bar association because he wasn't doing his job. He decided that he was going to get even. On my big day in court we negotiated a deal with the prosecutor in which she agreed. It was for a much lesser sentence than I received. The prosecutor explained to the judge why she was agreeing to the deal. Once Mr. Michael took the floor, he brought my criminal history up to the judge. She never asked him to explain anything about my history.  But my criminal history was the first thing that came out of his mouth. He read off every charge I ever had to the judge before she decided to make her decision. I have never seen nothing like it in my life. I felt super betrayed, it's almost like the prosecutor was my lawyer and Mr Michael had turned into the prosecution. Needless to say, I ended up with a much harsher sentence than what was agreed upon.  Everything I just explained are facts. If you're thinking about hiring Mr. Michael then go right ahead but you're better off taking your chances with a public defender. This is your warning, that's the experience I had with Mr. Michael. He might have gotten better over the years, who knows.
reviews.ownerResponse: I was appointed to represent Mr. Usher in 2008, at a time when he faced serious felony charges for alleged violent behavior.  Complicating the matter, he had a lengthy criminal record.  Ultimately, to avoid decades in prison, he wisely pled guilty and received a lesser sentence.  Two of his co-defendants did not make the same, intelligent decision, and they proceeded to trial, where they were found guilty and were sentenced to twice the period of incarceration as Mr. Usher.  My practice routinely involves representing individuals who, like Mr. Usher, commit serious offenses, which carry substantial punishments.  And, while I cannot spare all of them from jail time, I work zealously to protect their constitutional rights.  Such efforts have proved invaluable for thousands of clients, in my nearly 25 years of defending individuals accused of crimes.

reviews.text: Good lawyer Highly recommended. He communicates well and is a straight shooter. I seen reviews saying he's a racist and I can say I have never seen any indication of that. As a matter of fact upon my first initial visit to his office I was pleasantly surprised that the receptionist was a African American Lady his interaction with her was very warm and polite. and that made me comfortable.

Please do your research. My experience with Mr. Michaels was not the best and his avvo ratings seemed to be bought and paid. I gave him 10,000 to handle a case for my boyfriend and honestly feel as though a public defender would of been ten times better. He had a burglary charge and the other people involved got probation. My boyfriend got 7 years in jail. Never even had any priors.  He was not even as involved as the other people. I feel like he miss handled his case. He was not prepared the day of court and smelled like he was drinking. He assured us that he would be fine. We were not prepared for 7 yrs. All and all if I had a chance to do it over I definitely would not of hired him. I hired another lawyer for an appeal and they even said the deal my boyfriend received was crazy.
reviews.ownerResponse: “Ms. Wonder” is mistaken, as I have never charged anyone $10K for a burglary case.  I have, however, handled many cases involving such allegations, and not once, in 25 years of practice, has anyone received such a sentence as described above, nor were any of my clients treated as disproportionately to their co-defendants as alleged.  As for the “drinking” comment, I can only say that my courtroom demeanor is viewed as impeccable by my colleagues and judges alike.  As always, I am available to discuss any issues or concerns with current and past clients, and I welcome all constructive dialogues.

reviews.text: Used Mr. MIchael over a year ago on a referral from another attorney. Best thing we did was choosing Mr. Michael. I felt, and know he did way more than just what he was hired to do. Also to this day I can email him, and get a quick response. Wouldn't hesitate using again or blink when he mentions his retainer (which was more than fair.). Thanks Jem.

I hired Jim Michael on a serious felony charge a while back and he held my hand through the whole process he always stayed in contact and always returned my call. And the price I paid to retain him was very reasonable considering my case. He's very understanding and considerate he will work with you. Not to mention he's well known through judges and District Attorneys which in my opinion is helpful because at the end of the day "Its who you know" that determines certain outcomes

Do yourself a favor and don't hire this man. He's a racist and a bad lawyer I hired him to defend me and I wasn't satisfied so I ended up firing him he ended up going to the judge and having me locked up.

reviews.text: I had the pleasure of working very closely with Attorney Sarah Atkins and Paralegal Charmaine King-Smith, I imagine there are many more names behind the scenes but I got to speak with these two most often. In the beginning I had no idea what was possible I just knew my life as I knew was forever changed by illness. It is truly life changing to receive the call that we received a favorable decision and I will forever remember their support. For anyone struggling, PLEASE do not be afraid to reach out to an attorney, please know their IS help out there and you do not have to suffer alone. reviews.text: Was recommended these people by Atticus for help with filing for disability. Unless you are under current medical treatment at that exact moment they are unwilling to do anything, so unless it's a easy case that they charge just for sending paperwork don't bother just file yourself their a waste of time.
reviews.ownerResponse: Unfortunately, under Social Security disability law, the person claiming benefits is required to prove they are disabled and that is proven by medical evidence. So, if a claimant is not receiving medical treatment, there is no way we, or any other representative, can prove what the law requires to be proven and therefore we are unable to help. We are sorry we could not assist you. We try to help as many disabled people as we possibly can get the benefits that they are entitled to, but without medical evidence to prove disability, we are unable to do so.

reviews.text:
Very upset with the poor communication through out the process. I told Ibrahim, from the beginning, that I was not guilty and I intended to fight the charges. He assured me we would win, and we didn't.  He's over confident and very inexperienced and I think I would have done a better job myself and would have saved a lot of money in the process.

The most frustrating thing about the process was the lack of preparation on his part. He literally waited until the day prior to work on my case. I paid him $5,000 and saw him twice prior to trial. Both times it was about money. I don't think he cares about anything but being paid. I actually ran into the judge in the street, and he told me I had poor representation and that was the only reason I lost my case! His planning involved offering the officer on the stand a cup of water as if to say that he’s thoughtful when in reality none of that mattered. He said he  spent "hours captioning the audio on the dash cam video" but what he fails to mention is that all that was inadmissible because he failed to give it to the judge prior to trial so he can authenticate it!  Everyone was laughing at his inexperience and all he wanted to do was kiss up to the judge and assure him that he wouldn't try and appeal the verdict....thanks. I actually tried reaching out to him and it took him a month to get back to me after multiple attempts on my end only to get more empty promises and reasons why it was MY fault why we lost the case. That's the biggest thing I take issue with, It's how he lacks accountability. He'll always tells you reasons why things are never his fault. I'm not the type of person who likes to speak poorly about people but decided to because I looked at his reviews prior to hiring him and wished somebody would have taken a few minutes to warn me. Rest assured, the damage of hiring the wrong lawyer will stick with you for the rest of your life...so choose wisely! Be careful because businesses can pay to have your review removed.
reviews.ownerResponse: First of all, thank you for entrusting me with your case and having the courage of taking your case to trial. You never plead out and chose to fight, which takes guts. I do acknowledge and understand your dissatisfaction when we didn't receive the verdict we were praying for.  I, like you, believed in your case, and I spent weeks preparing and personally spent dozens of hours reviewing and captioning the audio on the dash cam video.  To this day, I think you were wrongly convicted.  I take every case extremely seriously and I completely understand your frustration.  Every client has my personal cell phone number and I almost always return calls/texts within 24 hours.  During a two and half day trial, I challenged every element of the state's case. I am deeply saddened that you still have these feelings against me for a trial that happened back in July of 2015, and I truly wish I can do something about it. Please call me at (706) 890-0000 if you'd like to discuss.

reviews.text: I really wish it was possible to give no stars. I had a horrible experience with law firm. I was referred by my chiropractor (whom I trust), who often told others about this law office in good faith, but I recently shared the details of my experience and requested her to please stop referring others to avoid the treatment that I have endured. I have had countless representatives regarding my case, a total of eight to be specific. All eight of them have left the company, leaving my case in limbo since 2020, but collected a check from the insurance company from the opposite party back in October 2022. I would have not known about this check if that insurance company did not send me a notification. Since receiving this check, Awad Law has refused to disperse the funds and return my phone calls to provide me an update. The last representative that left the company asked to represent me and I agreed. Awad Law withheld information from him and lied about my case and its content which ultimately impeded my case. After several months of back and forth the case expenses and fees were obtained and Awad law charged $22,000 of the $25,000 rewarded which is insane because they have done absolutely nothing! $22,000 is a very expensive headache. Since I have submitted a complaint to the bar and currently disputing the charges because this law firm is a scam. This is not a firm that I would recommend to anyone. If you have time to waste and money to loose, go for it, otherwise choose another law firm because you will not be treat with respect regarding your time and your case. Justice will NOT be severed here.

From the outset, Mrs. Clarke demonstrated exceptional information dissemination. She diligently kept me informed about my case, dedicating her efforts to securing a favorable outcome, even during an unfortunate period. I was recently involved in an automobile accident, and the attorneys at the Clarke Law Office were responsive and went out of their way to secure the most favorable  outcome possible. I highly recommend their services to anyone who wants a lawyer that is compassionate, professional and gets you the results you deserve!

During my initial phone conversation with Attorney Shanelle, I explained my husband’s case in detail, and she assured me that Attorney Clarke and their team could help. Proceeding to hear my case, asking me details over the phone, ensuring they could represent us and handle the case, and asked for my deposit — only for me to join a Zoom consultation the NEXT day, with Attorney’s Yemane and Shanelle just to be told I had “nothing worth representing.”

Throughout the hour-long call, I was repeatedly cut off while trying to explain my husband’s situation. I was told my case wasn’t valid because I lacked one form, and had “no evidence,” yet no one actually asked what evidence I had or took time to understand our relationship. When I asked what else would be needed if we moved forward, Attorney Clarke replied, “If you aren’t working with us, we can’t give you any advice.” The feeling of not knowing what to do, to spend your last on a consultation, to have professionals sit in your face and discredit your entire marriage, and relationship was one of the most belittling experiences I’ve ever experienced.  Luckily my father joined  the call and witnessed the entire interaction firsthand and was also very confused as to why they weren’t able to answer pretty much ANYTHING.

I found them online, and Based on their reviews online, and how positive the phone conversation was the day before, I expected professionalism and care — instead, it felt like they were more interested in collecting $250 than helping. Being dismissed, talked over, and made to feel like our relationship didn’t matter was disheartening and unprofessional. However, through another  Law Firm, my husband was granted bond not even months after this conversation — thank God!

I’ve been contemplating posting this review since June 19, when I first dealt with them. But now that  my husband was released on bond with the help of another attorney, I feel as though my experience could  help someone else. Despite the false hope and guarantee nothing could be done and it be successful, I am happy to say My Husband Is free ! Also that it’s very important to research WHO you are working with prior to giving them anything. I just hope and pray my experience doesn’t happen to anyone else. If this can prevent someone from losing their money or their hope, then please take HEED ! Maybe it was just a bad day for them, but this was my experience.
Good luck, everyone

reviews.text: Very Professional. Good listening ears. Wanted to help. Affordable. Highly recommend.

Clarke's Law Office was very professional and timely with our case. We seeked their help when our case was at its worst. In September 2019 I received a letter from USCIS giving me only 30 days in the US( my husband's and I petition for change of status was denied) and that was a month after I submitted more evidence upon their request (Notice of Intent To Deny). I gave them everything my husband and i had, from bank statements, Bills, car insurance, photographs with friends and family etc. We had initially filed our case on our own and without professional help. These were the exact same things we supplied Clarke's Law Office with when they started working on our case (Appeal/Re-open) in September and after 6 months of submission, we received a letter to say my husband's petition has been approved. In July 2020 we received another letter that my I-485 has also been approved. I received my green card in August in the mail. I felt overjoyed because all of this took place during Covid-19 season when everything seem to be failing. The Clarkes did not turn us away even though our case with USCIS was over 2 years old and and at that dark place where we thought we couldnt get a breakthrough. We were preparing for the worst. I am sharing these bits and pieces of my experience to say, do not take on these giants by yourselves, consult Clarke's Law office because they have the expertise to successfully handle your case. If you should come across this review, and is seeking help with immigration matters, please do not hesitate to contact Clarke's Law Office. I chose them because of reviews that I read on their website and I have now returned to say they are among the best you can find. They are very encouraging and understanding. Talking to them was a breeze. It felt just like you are talking to a good friend who will not judge or scorn you because of your situation. They provided us with the comfort and piece of mind through those 6-9 months that our case was being reviewed. I cannot thank them enough.
May they be a blessing to you as they were to my Family.

I would really recommend anyone with a need for an immigration attorney to visit the Clarke law office, we begun our process with an initial consultation visit and all our fears and anxiety were put to rest by attorney Clarke. Throughout the process we had constant communication, advice and guidance from the law office.

Once there, the door was locked so I rang the doorbell and waited patiently. After a few minutes I rang the doorbell again. There was a Toyota Corolla parked outside, and I could hear what sounded like people talking inside, there was also a CCTV camera pointed directly at the front door. Despite all these things, I was never greeted, let inside, or acknowledged whatsoever. I can't speak to the skills of this office  or its attorney's but from what brief experience I did have here, I would say to probably take your money elsewhere.

reviews.text:
Rich Jenkins ultimately wasted my families time assuring us we had a case and that he would help us much like you read on the other reviews here. After a month of waiting Rich set up an appointment to have all the evidence gathered which I had with me on my phone and rich seemed highly annoyed that I started with a photo proving circulation of a flyer printed about myself and stated this doesn’t prove it was circulated and then implied we were wasting his time because that one thing doesn’t prove anything but had rich not interrupted and had he listened instead of getting annoyed (probably because his internet and phones didnt work for his entire office) then he would have heard that I have everything he asked for. all of this information was saved in a folder on my phone because the items are detailed and Its quite a large folder also how do you print out videos further proving the circulation? I had this saved on my phone ready to be forwarded or printed out and had he not implied that my family member was to blame for not telling me to have all the things I had there for him today then maybe I would have saw fit to work with him. But after realizing he wanted this in printed form or otherwise I apologized for not understanding why we were there because what he wants is what I have and that still wasn’t even reviewed by him he waived his hand stood up and attacked my character and “attitude” for simply stating if we don’t have what he wants which we did.. then I don’t understand why we were there.

Rich ultimately spent less than 5 minutes to inflame the situation further and attack my character because he was having a bad day. To Rich personally, you are an attorney. You are not a disciple you are not a religious deity. You are an attorney and for you to treat People as lesser than you and then talk down to them and then blame their family members. You are everything that’s wrong with the justice system. No man will bow before you and from what I’ve seen- you would be prudent to adjust your anger and attitude accordingly because you have not the experience nor the understanding that is necessary to take cases with people who are affected to the core by what they’ve gone through. If this is how you treat people with traumatic brain injuries then you ought to get a new career or only take the easy cases- one look at you and we know what you’re gunna do- easy cases. This being an easy case I’m unsure as to what you even do other than become hostile because you weren’t clear with your communications. Also while sending him the emails of evidence in advance this attorney halted us stating  save it for the meeting as he was being overwhelmed. If multiple emails over a week or twos time is overwhelming then how are we to get you the evidence you need all in one meeting that you became hostile within five minutes of having? What an unprofessional, temperamental and hostile person you are. Good luck with your firm I can only imagine the good people you’ve turned away because you fail to communicate and because you jump to conclusions then attack the other side saying we aren’t listening- sir I had everything you asked for and then some and you didn’t review a single piece you waived it away and stood up ending the appointment like a scolded petulant child. Fix your internet fix your
Phones and quit blaming people for how they choose to store their evidence.

Laura.

That’s all I really need to say. LAURA.

But since I have a moment, let me add a little context.

I was searching for help with an employment issue and quickly learned a hard truth: when someone insists you come in just to talk—and attaches a $500 non-refundable consultation fee—that’s usually a sign they have zero confidence in your case and are perfectly fine squeezing a few more dollars out of someone who’s already dealing with a tough situation. I find that practice deplorable, but to each their own.

That is NOT Laura.

Laura spent 30 minutes actually listening to my situation. She gave me an honest assessment—no fluff, no pressure—and then went a step further. She clearly laid out what I should do next: A, B, and C. No invoice. No sales pitch. Just real guidance.

Who does that anymore?

If more attorneys operated with Laura’s level of integrity and professionalism, the legal profession wouldn’t carry the reputation it so often does.

So if you remember nothing else from this review, remember this:
LAURA.

Enough said.

reviews.text:
Mark and this team are life savers!

I want to start this review by stating that I’m an active member of the United States Army for 20 years. I was accused of things that I didn’t do all complete fabricated back in October of 21 by my ex wife. I was put through the wringer in my person life and my professional life for 3 years. Loss on income, security clearances, opportunities to advance in my career you name it.

Before finding Mark I had another lawyer who did absolutely nothing for me for 2 years. I didn’t even fully understand the charges I was facing. I started to fall into a dark place and losing hope. Until I called Mark Issa and gave him my story. He told me that he was going to fight for me and that he felt honored to do so.

Throughout this process Mark and his team became family to me  and they encouraged me that we’d come out on top. Mark Issa did everything he told me he would and more. I watched him work late nights compiling evidence and preparing me to testify. Once we went to trial I watched he and Mr Majeske work like lions in the court room. It was impressive to see. Once both sides rested the judge found me not guilty on all counts. After 3 and a half years my good name and reputation was cleared and restored all thanks to them. I highly recommend this law firm for any situation especially if it’s a criminal case.

I never felt left out or alone once this team was behind me. Mark you are the man! I can’t thank you and your firm enough. You and Mr. Majeske definitely have a seat at my dinner table anytime!
reviews.ownerResponse: What you have done for our country and what you have done as an individual made this worth every sleepless night preparing.  You are most welcome for the effort and I am glad we were able to obtain this result for you.  Jon and I have been doing this for a while together but it hardly gets easier. Thank you so much for the opportunity,

We would not recommend Michael S. Katz. We were experiencing issues with the construction of our home by builders Glen Sikes and Paul Cantrell, d/b/a DGI Enterprises, due to construction delays, defective workmanship, and liens being filed due to the non-payment of their contractors. In April 2009, we hired Mr. Katz to assist us in remedying the construction issues.  His first letter to the builders was sent on June 11, 2009. While we worked on obtaining estimates for home repairs and started working on those repairs, he refrained from pursuing the case, as he stated the damages were not fully ascertained. By 2011, the cost of repairs was fairly well established. As such, on April 25, 2011,he sent a letter to the opposing attorney regarding the case. Consequently, on September 13, 2011, he sent a letter to the builders advising them of the cost to complete the work needed on our home, a list of damages, and enclosed invoices associated with those cost. Although he sent this letter, he did not immediately move forward with arbitration. By March 2012, nothing further had been done to move the case forward. We were frustrated with the lack of progress and sent a certified letter to him. Despite this letter, he did not move forward with the arbitration request until six months later. Finally, on October 1, 2012, he filed a Demand for Arbitration. The Demand was not personally served on the builders until October 28, 2013, over a year later. On February 26, 2014, Arbitration notified he that the arbitration process had been initiated. On September 11, 2014, he sent a letter to arbitration, advising that we were ready to move forward with the proceeding and requesting that the arbitration be scheduled. Meanwhile, on October 4, 2014, he researched the statute of limitations on negligent construction claims. On June 25, 2015, an arbitration hearing was set for October 26, 2015. On November 4, 2015, the Arbitrator signed the scheduling order, which required that all discovery be completed by February 12, 2016. The order also set the arbitration for March 28, 2016. On November 18, 2015, the opposing attorney set the depositions for us on December 4, 2015. The week before those depositions, Mr. Katz notified him that he had a criminal case conflict for the date, taking us off the calendar. The rescheduling of the depositions was set for January 2016. We were not notified of the rescheduling. Throughout this time, Arbitration sent regular invoices to all parties. On December 23, 2015, a case manager with arbitration emailed both attorneys seeking payment of outstanding invoices and gave a deadline by February 15, 2016. Mr. Katz did not pay by February 15 and never responded to arbitration. Arbitration closed its file on our claim on April 26, 2016. The arbitration firm deemed the arbitration dismissed or abandoned on that date. After the dismissal of the arbitration, he did nothing further to pursue our case. On October 26, 2016, the six-month statute to refile our claim expired. Additionally, Georgia's 8-year statute of repose on negligent construction claims expired on June 17, 2017. As a result of his failure to refile the claim prior to the expiration of the renewal statute and the statute of repose, we are now precluded from ever recovering on our claim. On June 29, 2018, over two years after dismissal, he sent us a letter withdrawing from the representation. Mr. Katz never notified us about his decision to dismiss and/or abandon the arbitration. He made the decision unilaterally, despite it was contrary to our instructions. We did file a grievance with the State Bar of Georgia and in July of 2020, The Board determined that Mr. Katz violated Rule 1.4 of the Georgia Rules of Professional Conduct and issued a Formal Letter of Admonition for his misconduct. This level of discipline prohibits the State Bar from revealing information about this case to the public. However, this is part of his permanent record and if he violates the disciplinary rules again, the Formal Letter of Admonition may be considered by the Supreme Court.

I have to admit I was not the easiest client to deal with at times. Thank God he is very layed back level headed person and understands what extremely emotional parents are going through at this difficult time.

I feel as Mr. Katz is now my family’s attorney and will continue to use him if he lets me!!

My experience with this attorney wasn't very good.  I never actually met him in person but only dealt with him over the phone.  He was biased against me as a client.  Mr. Katz is based out of Decatur and seemed disconnected to the overall Atlanta market.
Saved me 500$! Polite and professional Attorney with a plethora of experience and knowledge.

I have hired Mr Katz several times for DUI's I've had in the past. Was very pleased with the outcome he had 2 DUI's dismissed for me he always worked with me on a pair plan and very professional and experienced in court he will get you the best deal he can get your DUI dropped to a wreckles driving  and less possible jail time or none at all. If you're looking for an attorney  for a DUI you got Mr Katz is your man. Thanks! Mr Katz for being there for me.

I was arrested in Gwinntt Co. and retained Mr.  Katz for $4,000 for 3 counties to represent me. Look this Attorney avoided all phone calls even with family member.  Mr.  Katz would not file for me a bond. So that I could get released from jail.  Michael Katz is NOT a good Attorney and he will run off with you money.  Shop around MR. KATZ is a crook!

I was in an accident recently (truck pulled out in front of me very early in the morning... my car was totalled and I required surgery on both hands... i had heard from several 'ambulance chasing lawyers and I did not feel iike that had my best insterest in mind... Upon discussing with my orthopedic surgeon he whole-heartedly referred Steve Ozcomert... I was never more pleased with the service and results that I received...
My medical bills were not huge .. but I need to have them covered and assist me in gaining enough funds to replace me car (without having to buy a junker) and to also reimburse time lost at work and cover the 'hassle' of the situation...
By far the 'greatest', asset the Steve has in his arsenal is his paralegal (Tatum)... she was always available to talk to .. and answered all questions (no matter how menial) just to insure that I understood the process and how it was going...
I recommend Steve very highly... and I promise that you will  be as satisfied as I was for this entire process..

The Ozcomert Law Firm is the premier personal injury firm in Atlanta!  Stephen and his staff provide the highest degree of legal expertise and professionalism no matter how big or small your case may be.  I would recommend the Ozcomert Law Firm to my friends and family for its personal injury needs.

I have had a pleasant experience with attorney Stephen Ozcomert’s office. He has represented me in the past and I knew that he would be the right person as an injury attorney. He worked diligently to get the best results regarding my case. The paralegal Tatum is always pleasant and informed me about the details of my case. The communication was great and I had a positive experience.

If you're searching for an injury lawyer who genuinely prioritizes your well-being, you've found the right legal team. I first met Stephen Ozcomert in 2020 when my son was involved in a terrible car accident. As the world was shutting down due to the COVID-19 outbreak, Stephen came to the hospital just hours before lockdowns took effect to meet with my family and discuss my son’s injuries.

Throughout the case, his paralegal, Tatum, kept us informed every step of the way and regularly checked in on my son’s recovery. Stephen and his team never pressured us into any decisions we weren’t comfortable with and were always available to answer our questions.

Fast forward to 2022—I was in a car accident myself, and without hesitation, I knew exactly who to call. Once again, Tatum was amazing, just as I expected. Clear and concise communication is very important to me, and this team consistently delivers on that. Before and after my surgery, Tatum checked in on my recovery, ensuring I was supported every step of the way.

I highly recommend this law office and have even referred friends who have found themselves in similar situations. If you need a team that truly cares, look no further.

Steve is one of the best personal injury attorneys I have ever known.  I have had him take the lead on several personal injury cases over the years.  I was first introduced to him by my father almost 20 years ago.  He knows the law as well as anyone in the area.  He is always responsive to his clients.  He is honest and trustworthy.  I can think of three cases with him in the last two years that resulted in settlements 4 times more than whaat I thought they were worth, and two of those cases were close to a million dollars.  Any lawyer on tv would have settled for less than half in my opinion.  He is kind, compassionate, and a bulldog when the other side isn't playing fair.  He is just genuinely phenomenal at what his does, because he is a genuine person who cares about his job and the people he represents.
One of the best and most reputable attorneys in Georgia for serious injury representation. Highly recommended!

Living in Atlanta can be crazy on the road. I have been hit several times. I was referred to Steve by a friend and I am glad they did! Although, no amount of money can heal certain injuries from accidents, two policy limits later I am glad to have met Steve and Tatum. Not only did I trust them with my cases, but I sent multiple friends and family as well! I highly recommend!!!

I had an accident towards the end of August.  My friend referred me to Stephen Ozcomert && I’m glad she did. I had the pleasure of working with Tatum. She was sweet (over the phone and in person) and she was prompt with communication. I would refer this law firm to anybody who need it!

They helped me out in the past both with traffic tickets and a collision. I met directly with Mr. Ozcomert, and he was very knowledgeable and friendly. He made sure I was taken care of and did all of the heavy lifting. He was upfront about what to expect and never made any false promises.

I've been dealing with Steven Ozcomert.... For at least 8 years or more.They are a bunch of understanding amazing wonderful people. They get five stars all the way around especially Tatum... Just would like to thank you guys

I have been privileged to know Mr. Ozcomert for about 15 years.  My personal experience with him has been nothing less than superb. He is a highly intellectual and knowledgable attorney who maintains a personal and approachable spirit. He truly puts a client’s interest first.
I have recommended Mr. Ozcomert to many people and it’s an honor to put my own reputation on collateral when I do.

I had the pleasure of working with Tatum from the initial consultation to the resolution of my case. She consistently demonstrated exceptional knowledge, responsiveness, and support throughout the entire process. Tatum made sure I was well-informed and confident in my decisions.

I highly recommend Stephen M. Ozcomert to anyone needing reliable expert legal representation. His professionalism and commitment to achieving the best possible outcome were genuinely commendable. I am so grateful.

I was injured 3 years ago and Steve Ozcomert took excellent care of me. Steve is extremely knowledgeable, caring, communicative and honest. I am thankful for his expertise and that he believed in me and he battled for me. I can see why he has received Super Lawyer so many times because he's my Super Lawyer. His paralegal Anne is truly a compassionate, intelligent, hard-working person and I appreciate having with her through this rough time in my life. Thank you guys for all you have done.

N.A.

After being involved in a car accident last year, In which I was injured greatly.  I was referred to Mr. Ozcomet. I must say, I am blessed and very fortunate to have had Steve and Anne to handle my personal injury case. They were professional and efficient from beginning to ending. They are truly trustworthy, truthful, honest, kind and compassionate individuals, As We know these qualities are often missing in many, in our world today. I highly recommend Steve and Anne especially to my family, friends and anyone who are in need of excellent legal services, Thank You for all that You did to make a life changing experience bearable. You guys are the Best!

I was in an auto accident in July 2012.  Atty Steve and Anne are easy to work with and they get the job done.  Anne kept me updated on the process of my claim and I never had to wonder what the status was.  If she had any questions she always called and when I had questions she always returned my call immediately.  I highly recommend them.  Anne also made sure I received the medical care I needed. Great firm and great people.

Atty Ozcomert and his paralegal, Anne Trapp, provide great and personal client service.  They make sure you understand the good, bad and ugly of your case.  This firm has successfully settled two cases for my family and at least one for a referral of mine.  I highly recommend Atty Ozcomert to family & friends.
Jerry and Harriet were knowledgeable, professional, concerned, and most importantly patient throughout my legal process. Highly recommended!

I am very pleased to submit my strongest possible recommendation for the excellence of Family-related legal services provided to me by Mr. Jerry L. Stepp, P.C. Mr. Stepp and his very capable staff helped me in contested divorce proceedings, and in establishing child support on behalf of my 12-year-old triplets. He also helped me to prepare to my satisfaction a Last Will and Testament and an Advanced Health Directive. I can't imagine that any other attorney could have been more helpful.

Jerry helped me resolve a significant issue with my landlord when the property owner failed to obtain a certificate of occupancy prior to renting me a townhouse in a new development.  I was impressed by the quick response from the property owner once Jerry was involved!

Mr. Stepp is extremely knowledgeable! The diversity of his experience as a lawyer is an advantage that gives his clients an edge. Mr. Stepp's ability to respond and strategize taking into consideration all facets of my various situations over the years has always led to positive outcomes, every, single, time.           Colette

Jerry helped me sort out a auto mechanic issue that had taken up the better half of my time and energy in 2015. He is professional and fair priced, but above all, understanding, patient, and sympathetic. Kill them with kindness, they say, and there's no one better to do the job. Jerry and Harriett make a great team!

Jerry and Harriett were wonderful throughout our legal ordeal.
They allowed us to focus on recovering from our injuries caused by the accident while they focused on making sure we were taken care of financially.
They were caring and professional and we would highly recommend them to anyone who needs a personal injury attorney.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you, Gus, for your review.  We were happy to be able to assist you and Lisa with your accident. We are so glad that we were able to bring this matter to a very positive resolution for you both.
